name: Justin Streiff, Turning Point USA
subject: The campus climate just turned vicious...
body:
| | | [](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=8c906e67d792495f8599360a29162a77&_e=KmX3b7KTOM2eVXQouy1BSb395-Z2Hpq-Kb19vjyjlnPydg_M41lDq6XwEVDcaIGrQuJXfffNxBFkvcT9PbzA3-_R2YlEILKLGyut5FPx6Aqu2HQyTGQyqxxDKcTs9kP_4eBcRumsnLVoICtmqPvRH_AKAoykJpJCSnFizbdCnL8mHHjaVsUf3LN84JPYzAoLwM5wpBJTYkozp8Wk6OX17nxx4axY7yjuwoEsdprV6i5JpmrS9l-VYMbZOzKUMRWA54QmEzNKq6dfe38OMG4sTds0RgYsPyPU8grVJPmb2Gh3bcvwWPL9eZwiN5_xJ1If0miunEnv5QGCWCDy7gZ0ZxgsSNpI6IqiBucJDaVHGSBzL9hvHTblZzZ7xkCkUwQ3HV_LrND7-OLBmvEhIOrwFPcS4ioPb0lnN7D5XRQVaQkleZuyci9tbnmEY-V5ALo65Pg_qsAOycKQ03k-zfhKIF6V6dLMnpzxBJjRpGZwIVyLdoyk0z5DXeTix4pXCN-lx7-cRVHwea1IxILSWlHgkw8hmRYreDWDRGVfe1Kr7ExOa2YzDkfz84dQT7ZGxLd7vG-VxoJ8BoamxHxYwcMNe_zWySxziYEEhDW2zdt6H-e6NykWxL8DyI3qzdM36bXSoRdCCMM4d3nM98V4Dt8GWiljk3qAieRLu9dijs_MrZy56z2PVolqvSpV7Zb049sq_wggVRyWkcfmSZ3YxSzqNA%3D%3D)
---
| | Patriot,
---
I have to say it...

The campus climate this spring?

_It 's been downright ugly._

To be frank, the Left has never made it easy for our students. That's always come with the territory when you're speaking truth deep in the belly of the beast...

But this semester is different.

**This has been one of the most hostile semesters Turning Point USA students have ever faced. **

From coast to coast, high school and college students who dared to stand for faith, freedom, and this great nation have been stalked, slandered, threatened, and in some cases -- _physically attacked. _

All for saying what you and I know to be true:

That America is worth defending. That speech must remain free. And that Charlie Kirk's life and legacy meant something.

And despite the opposition -- our students didn't back down.

They didn't run. They didn't hide.

They showed up -- earlier, louder, and stronger than ever before.

_But not without cost. _

**Let me tell you more about what our students have been up against so far this year... **

In Maryland, one of our high school leaders -- just 17 years old -- helped organize a Club America event on campus back in December.

It was peaceful. Students only. Parental permission required. No outside adults. Everything done by the book.

And yet, weeks later, during a recent local school board meeting...

A local woman stood up and publicly accused this student of "grooming children."

Then she went even further.

She told the board: _" All members of this Board are mandatory reporters... Based on the circumstances surrounding this event, **a report was made to Child Protective Services. " **_

Yes, you read that right -- she weaponized CPS against one of our students.

Filed against a **minor. **

For organizing a pro-America Turning Point USA event with his friends.

Now, I want you to imagine being 17 years old... doing everything right... following every rule... showing up with courage and conviction...

And then hearing a stranger stand up in front of your entire community and imply that you're a danger to children.

That you need to be investigated. That you're the problem.
---
[](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=8c906e67d792495f8599360a29162a77&_e=KmX3b7KTOM2eVXQouy1BSb395-Z2Hpq-Kb19vjyjlnPydg_M41lDq6XwEVDcaIGrQuJXfffNxBFkvcT9PbzA3-_R2YlEILKLGyut5FPx6Aqu2HQyTGQyqxxDKcTs9kP_4eBcRumsnLVoICtmqPvRH_AKAoykJpJCSnFizbdCnL8mHHjaVsUf3LN84JPYzAoLwM5wpBJTYkozp8Wk6OX17nxx4axY7yjuwoEsdprV6i5JpmrS9l-VYMbZOzKUMRWA4TCdDbLUXXBcy1i45a3baT5VNZ6xuaT8Jw3h5lEKmLK8pQYZgHBEGYrCFbzYIWlNzFPvbOF5rmgc2kn1Y_wnQPVxYdOPpJHOvCIXdY_Nj2KLGyXroW7dYlTJjOIGO80i4_vi0izCx8p0iCaaGgUQzixuz53vX2kMueYNF5rc-QHliGfvme2RlQcmN2q1NIYS1WAXhmFn-dYS3TBSj-Esi2Fx8nfQN4zBC-h93xdYIQjEcpUlJesXogY0XWMQVIVE9JZdzF6lvK_nPy1PyrkT5MSeQe1h-un4xETGmImrWBaBKVz95lE6AZIWr33xXf_N4YANmYD8XbPt7be3ZtGb7qGHndw7FEcue7RfUhWy2fd4KyPs2T53GaoNRsJSnLibHyL47MBjVIoGvz_HoQ7d-Isjs5vweE3zRphOnv4GyGqSYTe_a7B9uqQoMv8_1YD92sApXdJUK2z0fPywH4uoTA%3D%3D)
---
_**Above:** "Nancy" -- the Leftist Karen who filed a CPS report against a local Club America chapter and spoke out against them at a recent school board meeting. _
---
How would _you_ respond?

Because I'll tell you what he did...

He walked up to that microphone -- calm, steady, unshaken -- and said:

_" We have been accused of many things... I'm the president of the group. I'm 17, I'm a minor. I can't groom children because I am a child." _

Seventeen years old. Standing alone. But standing strong in front of his entire community while facing outrageous, malicious accusations.

That's courage. The kind of courage Charlie would've been proud of.

And that courage is catching fire across the country. _But so is the hatred. _

Because what happened in Maryland wasn't an isolated incident... It's just the beginning:

🚨 **At Lawrence University in Wisconsin, Leftists on campus are trying to permanently shut down a brand-new TPUSA chapter before it even starts. **

Never mind that Lawrence has over 160 student clubs... and not one is right-leaning. Or that the university handbook bans discrimination based on political affiliation.

One of our students there is now receiving death threats.

And instead of protecting him?

The Left-wing student government is putting the chapter's fate up to a campus-wide vote.

🚨 **At Iowa State University, **Colby Brandt -- the student body president -- was threatened with _**impeachment**_ because he had connections to TPUSA. Fortunately, his "impeachment" was dismissed by the student government's judicial branch... But this was only after the outrage that followed on social media and the university's administration came to its senses.
---

clean_body: | | | | | Patriot, I have to say it... The campus climate this spring? It 's been downright ugly. To be frank, the Left has never made it easy for our students. That's always come with the territory when you're speaking truth deep in the belly of the beast... But this semester is different. This has been one of the most hostile semesters Turning Point USA students have ever faced. From coast to coast, high school and college students who dared to stand for faith, freedom, and this great nation have been stalked, slandered, threatened, and in some cases -- physically attacked. All for saying what you and I know to be true: That America is worth defending. That speech must remain free. And that Charlie Kirk's life and legacy meant something. And despite the opposition -- our students didn't back down. They didn't run. They didn't hide. They showed up -- earlier, louder, and stronger than ever before. But not without cost. Let me tell you more about what our students have been up against so far this year... In Maryland, one of our high school leaders -- just 17 years old -- helped organize a Club America event on campus back in December. It was peaceful. Students only. Parental permission required. No outside adults. Everything done by the book. And yet, weeks later, during a recent local school board meeting... A local woman stood up and publicly accused this student of "grooming children." Then she went even further. She told the board: " All members of this Board are mandatory reporters... Based on the circumstances surrounding this event, a report was made to Child Protective Services. " Yes, you read that right -- she weaponized CPS against one of our students. Filed against a minor. For organizing a pro-America Turning Point USA event with his friends. Now, I want you to imagine being 17 years old... doing everything right... following every rule... showing up with courage and conviction... And then hearing a stranger stand up in front of your entire community and imply that you're a danger to children. That you need to be investigated. That you're the problem. Above: "Nancy" -- the Leftist Karen who filed a CPS report against a local Club America chapter and spoke out against them at a recent school board meeting. How would you respond? Because I'll tell you what he did... He walked up to that microphone -- calm, steady, unshaken -- and said: " We have been accused of many things... I'm the president of the group. I'm 17, I'm a minor. I can't groom children because I am a child." Seventeen years old. Standing alone. But standing strong in front of his entire community while facing outrageous, malicious accusations. That's courage. The kind of courage Charlie would've been proud of. And that courage is catching fire across the country. But so is the hatred. Because what happened in Maryland wasn't an isolated incident... It's just the beginning: 🚨 At Lawrence University in Wisconsin, Leftists on campus are trying to permanently shut down a brand-new TPUSA chapter before it even starts. Never mind that Lawrence has over 160 student clubs... and not one is right-leaning. Or that the university handbook bans discrimination based on political affiliation. One of our students there is now receiving death threats. And instead of protecting him? The Left-wing student government is putting the chapter's fate up to a campus-wide vote. 🚨 At Iowa State University, Colby Brandt -- the student body president -- was threatened with impeachment because he had connections to TPUSA. Fortunately, his "impeachment" was dismissed by the student government's judicial branch... But this was only after the outrage that followed on social media and the university's administration came to its senses. 🚨 At Worcester Polytechnic, one of our signs that featured a quote with Charlie was vandalized with the phrase: "he should've ducked ahole." 🚨 At Lake Zurich High School, one of our students held an "I ❤️ ICE" sign -- and was physically threatened by a peer. On school grounds and caught on camera. 🚨 At the University of Houston, a Leftist mocked Charlie Kirk's assassination while harassing TPUSA students at a tabling event on campus -- wishing cancer and paralysis on their families while making sexual gestures. 🚨 And at Coastal Carolina Community College, a professor was recorded saying this in front of his class: "Did he (Charlie Kirk) deserve to die? No... but..." Then he added: " He was a racist piece of st. And Turning Point USA is a racist piece of st organization." The message was clear. I could go on and on with more examples of the Left's hatred towards our students... But while they mock Charlie's death, encourage hate, harassment, and try to poison students against the very ideas our country was built on... In the face of all this -- our students aren 't folding. Not one bit. They're still tabling at campus events. They're still meeting after school -- still recruiting, still growing. They're still passing out pocket Constitutions and American flags. They're still launching chapters in hostile territory -- places where they've been told, outright, that they're not welcome. And while their peers stage "ICE Walkouts" and chaos in the halls... Our students are gathering to pray -- for their schools, for law enforcement, and for this nation -- quietly around their school's flagpole. That's because these students made a decision -- the same decision Charlie challenged them to every day: " It doesn't take skill to be courageous. It takes a choice." They chose courage. They chose to stand for faith, freedom, and family. They chose to defend everything that makes this country worth fighting for. They truly are the courageous generation. In fact, since Charlie 's assassination last September: 🔥 Over 140,000 students raised their hands to get involved 🔥 4,600+ active chapters are now operating across America 🔥 More than 1 million students are part of this movement And Patriot, patriots like you are the reason they've been able to do it... Because while the Left mocked Charlie's death-- while the hate followed and the attacks intensified -- you didn't disappear. Patriots like you stood firm. You stood with these students. You made it clear: The mission doesn 't end here. And now, because of your faithfulness, we 're watching the seeds Charlie planted come to life in high schools, colleges, and communities across this country. Our students may not know your name... But they know someone made it possible for them to take a stand on their campus. They're walking into classrooms bolder than ever. Speaking truth in places where lies once ruled. Because someone gave them the chance. That someone was you. Patriots like you reminded these students that they're not alone -- even when it feels like it. You helped push back the darkness. You made space for courage to grow. The kind of courage Charlie lived for. The kind of courage he gave everything for. So, thank you, Patriot. Thank you for giving these young patriots a fighting chance. We will not stop. We will not retreat. We will keep standing for truth -- louder and stronger than ever before. Because Charlie's mission didn't end with his life. It's being carried forward -- by thousands of students... and by you. And that 's the one thing the Left can't stand. Let's keep pushing. Let's keep growing. Let's keep going -- together. | | For Charlie, Justin Streiff Chief Operating OfficerTurning Point USA P.S. Patriot, thank you again for taking a stand for our brave students. It's truly incredible watching these students from across the country lining up to start the next wave of new Turning Point chapters. They're watching what's happening... seeing the attacks... hearing the hate from the Left... And still saying: " Sign me up." All they need? A little backup. Every new chapter receives a full activism kit from Turning Point USA, filled with everything they need to lead boldly: ✅ Pocket Constitutions ✅ American Flags ✅ Chapter Leadership Tools ✅ Organizing Guides ✅ Patriotic TPUSA Gear Each one costs just $25 to send to a student chapter. You don't need to start a chapter yourself. But you can help a new one get off the ground. And you can be the reason a kid on a hostile campus doesn't feel like they're fighting alone. ** 4940 East Beverly Road Phoenix, Arizona 85044

I didn’t run for Senate to sit quietly on the sidelines. I’m a mom, a conservative, and a proud Alabamian—and I came to fight for the values that make America strong.

_**Faith. Family. Freedom.**_

The Left is doing _everything_ they can to tear it all down. But I didn’t come to Washington to play games. I came to fight for the people who feel forgotten, overlooked, and ignored.

From securing the border to protecting our kids and defending our way of life—I’m in this for the long haul.

**This fight isn’t easy, but it’s worth it. And I’m not going anywhere.**

Will you stand with me?
---

I put my full faith in people like you, Peter, but now I’m starting to wonder if I should have been more worried. \- Katie I didn’t run for Senate to sit quietly on the sidelines. I’m a mom, a conservative, and a proud Alabamian—and I came to fight for the values that make America strong. Faith. Family. Freedom. The Left is doing everything they can to tear it all down. But I didn’t come to Washington to play games. I came to fight for the people who feel forgotten, overlooked, and ignored. From securing the border to protecting our kids and defending our way of life—I’m in this for the long haul. This fight isn’t easy, but it’s worth it. And I’m not going anywhere. Will you stand with me? Paid for by Britt for Alabama, Inc.

clean_body: | | Peter, I'm conducting an Official SOTU Poll after President Trump's historic address to the nation. This unprecedented initiative was authorized by JD Vance and Chairman Gruters - and only the absolute best of the best are being invited to take part. **** I'm hoping to share your responses directly with the President next time I see him. And I just know that he would LOVE to hear what you thought about his speech. **** Together, we will MAGA! Pollster Kellyanne Conway Paid for by the Republican National Committee Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

You probably didn't hear about this. _And that 's a shame. _

Because it's a HUGE win.

A win for Utah. A win for America. A win for all grassroots patriots.

_And a win that proves something bigger ... _

**The Turning Point Action machine works.**

Let me back up and give you the full story...

Back in 2018, the Left convinced Utah's voters to (barely) approve a cleverly worded ballot initiative named Prop 4.

To voters, it sounded harmless._It wasn 't. _

Prop 4 took redistricting power away from elected lawmakers and voters... and handed it to an unelected "independent" commission.

_Translation? _

A backdoor way to carve out a Left-wing seat in Congress in deep-red Utah. A quiet map-rigging operation.

And for weeks, the headlines screamed that the effort to repeal Prop 4 was doomed to fail.

_" They don't have the numbers"... "They can't get enough signatures"... "It's too late." _

That's what they said.

But the Left and their parrots in the media forgot something...

They forgot about the patriots in Utah who had already been working diligently for months. They forgot about the grassroots.

**And they forgot about what happens when Turning Point Action dives into a fight. **
---
[](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=57fe604afc094510a42f9b31431d26c5&_e=AiiVkv5mumfwIjuPqrxYi2vMxEQOs64iLGkrSwiiWuvrqeL8DoOvCsrw8szWc8kh1DY6hh0T9z8A0dnw5ECui4w6x9QPK6Et4EOhjyE6xZ-Qrn6rEKe92zti2O7By2gTSNBaYqPNcftEKn5s_JLEXqZ4PWPoARzLksjkEzzOxNWy91Jka4vGDfoWZcQvIaoXxSLoKKOSy2U1fBhzOjW8dioGaMrbw5NeEPRdTbCNzqyCVLspr3T7XFdYqOLD-TvdouzZoYCu1s7oFEeN25RgRofq8E9ZwbNqKFPOnwHAqYzqcrzACMbxMvC_l7IpH5PddrFS3yOv0QRkJDxZyMiRYhW6Ivyp-XSSuay7N3WhDeDtqyM2ktDerZ8wmWyXeRviW2gklV6JmZ9NzHPt1emayk486byXiMXlaN_FmvlAJu0A6sciTShxQMiIOhFDLnm0M9_0Mvxw7PK_sl5BpdMLcde4G3xSb2OycKHYkHnMsfl6gEQtDUYBfbZA3-j09MX5Nvb9COXuSdkU-h8Akj7OShx50mp38fMy7tTElpUcOasZo1burgcrn1YswS_wtezherrd5_NrNk7iPMpws9WRLw1TUy4FKJQu4KrxTYtHHDO26Pya6lhHjhxcOponS9HM_-Wh1e2NCMK_JpEpASJiewZ9jshqVRG5P2lzsL9RkNE%3D)

**In just a matter of weeks, the combined effort of the conservative groups on the ground in Utah and Turning Point Action turned in over 200,000 signatures... **

Well above the 141,000 signature threshold needed to place the repeal of Prop 4 on the ballot.

We didn't just hit it. _We blew past it. _

To be clear, signatures are still being verified. And the Left is already working overtime to pressure signers to remove their signature and sabotage the repeal effort.

Because they know what this means.

_If voters approve this repeal? _

The Left's plan to carve out seats in Congress from red states will be foiled.

_And that terrifies them. _

**But here 's what matters most, Peter... **

Turning Point Action was one of many groups in this fight. And we showed up when the fight called us to Utah.

We launched SEVEN Super Chase events. Trained volunteers. Knocked doors. And worked hand-in-hand with the Utah patriots who carried the load.

This was teamwork. This was hustle. This was the movement doing what it does best.

And it's proof of concept.

Because this... THIS...

Is what the machine Charlie built can do.

When we stay disciplined. When we stick to the strategy. When we outwork and out-hustle the Left.

**We win. Period. **

_Now imagine this, Peter ... _

We were on the ground in Utah for a mere couple of weeks.

_Weeks. _

And look what happened.

_What happens if we build NOW for 2026? _

_What happens if we chase ballots NOW?_

**What happens if we train hundreds more ballot chasers in Arizona ... Nevada... New Hampshire... in every critical battleground across the country? **

The same math applies.

Knock more doors + Chase more ballots = Win more races (and save America).

We're training new ballot chasers every day at Turning Point Action HQ in Arizona.

We've already established offices in Nevada, New Hampshire, and other battlegrounds to lock down the new RED WALL.

We just need to make sure we stay ahead of the Left to beat them at their own game.

Because the Left never does.

They'll regroup. Adapt. Copy what we've built. _**Peter, we need you on the front lines with us now more than ever. **_

Like I mentioned, we're training new patriots to join the ranks of our ballot-chasing army every day.

We're giving them the tools and strategies to do the hard work on the ground -- _door by door. _

clean_body: A huge win you should know about...| | | Peter, You probably didn't hear about this. And that 's a shame. Because it's a HUGE win. A win for Utah. A win for America. A win for all grassroots patriots. And a win that proves something bigger ... The Turning Point Action machine works. Let me back up and give you the full story... Back in 2018, the Left convinced Utah's voters to (barely) approve a cleverly worded ballot initiative named Prop 4. To voters, it sounded harmless.It wasn 't. Prop 4 took redistricting power away from elected lawmakers and voters... and handed it to an unelected "independent" commission. Translation? A backdoor way to carve out a Left-wing seat in Congress in deep-red Utah. A quiet map-rigging operation. And for weeks, the headlines screamed that the effort to repeal Prop 4 was doomed to fail. " They don't have the numbers"... "They can't get enough signatures"... "It's too late." That's what they said. But the Left and their parrots in the media forgot something... They forgot about the patriots in Utah who had already been working diligently for months. They forgot about the grassroots. And they forgot about what happens when Turning Point Action dives into a fight. In just a matter of weeks, the combined effort of the conservative groups on the ground in Utah and Turning Point Action turned in over 200,000 signatures... Well above the 141,000 signature threshold needed to place the repeal of Prop 4 on the ballot. We didn't just hit it. We blew past it. To be clear, signatures are still being verified. And the Left is already working overtime to pressure signers to remove their signature and sabotage the repeal effort. Because they know what this means. If voters approve this repeal? The Left's plan to carve out seats in Congress from red states will be foiled. And that terrifies them. But here 's what matters most, Peter... Turning Point Action was one of many groups in this fight. And we showed up when the fight called us to Utah. We launched SEVEN Super Chase events. Trained volunteers. Knocked doors. And worked hand-in-hand with the Utah patriots who carried the load. This was teamwork. This was hustle. This was the movement doing what it does best. And it's proof of concept. Because this... THIS... Is what the machine Charlie built can do. When we stay disciplined. When we stick to the strategy. When we outwork and out-hustle the Left. We win. Period. Now imagine this, Peter ... We were on the ground in Utah for a mere couple of weeks. Weeks. And look what happened. What happens if we build NOW for 2026? What happens if we chase ballots NOW? What happens if we train hundreds more ballot chasers in Arizona ... Nevada... New Hampshire... in every critical battleground across the country? The same math applies. Knock more doors + Chase more ballots = Win more races (and save America). We're training new ballot chasers every day at Turning Point Action HQ in Arizona. We've already established offices in Nevada, New Hampshire, and other battlegrounds to lock down the new RED WALL. We just need to make sure we stay ahead of the Left to beat them at their own game. Above Left: It's blurry, but that's me leading a training session for Chase the Vote patriots bound for New Hampshire. Above Right: A recent class of graduates who are already hitting the pavement to chase ballots. We simply can't take our foot off the gas for one moment. We can't take anything for granted. Because the Left never does. They'll regroup. Adapt. Copy what we've built. Peter, we need you on the front lines with us now more than ever. Like I mentioned, we're training new patriots to join the ranks of our ballot-chasing army every day. We're giving them the tools and strategies to do the hard work on the ground -- door by door. How to talk to voters. How to get another ballot in the box. How to win. Every $50 you give to Turning Point Action today helps sponsor another ballot chaser 's training and deploys them where it matters most -- immediately after they graduate. I want you to know this: patriots like you made success in Utah possible. You stood with us. You believed in the machine. You helped build it. And I don't take that lightly. I'm grateful. Truly. Now, let's take this momentum and use it to build the fiercest, fastest, and most effective ballot-chasing machine America has ever seen. Our country's future and this movement's future depends on it. For Charlie, Tyler Bowyer Chief Operating OfficerTurning Point Action P.S. The headlines said it couldn't be done. The pundits rolled their eyes. The Left smirked... And then 200,000+ signatures said otherwise. That's what happens when patriots show up. Now let's show up in the battlegrounds that matter for 2026 and beyond. We've proved the machine Charlie built works. That it WINS. So, let's build early. Chase early. Win early. name: Rudy Giuliani
subject: I’m counting on you, plain and simple
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rudy Defense Trust
body:
This is the only way to make me whole...
This is the only way to make me whole...
| Peter,
---
This is what it looked like after the Democrats' goons arrested and booked me in one of the most violent jails in America...
---
[ ](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=ccdbc258add34428af3d11b1c8f95216&_e=9_ObjDCgfANdB1KtUt_X3dY1MDfZK-4zzqr1XXkc7q2oi09XCQ9GFjX0kkrpIT5vaYCfxpEyDyYk13m0jSU8AwYr8FURKACSxEgaHFSplByf9vSaT2N836aGCYSqD7WjrjvD3QgDiJEKWOSd82E-nBI4N2ZQJZ8dMypLc3RYUq5J9w0PRONhhucmBso0xsMHGG9kSh7PaFGsj773jS9is-sg7CjZH1qgzD_X9r776_Sik46kS9SAU4OzFN8W635ELeZGmWrsw-RhU2FJvYFnJGJski9hDiLjYsy5fNYIDVxXcd8RpXsoLVmUGVAHQXRFSnAa-8lprsDS7QzDmaEXmh35eovfnafwxA9yMajRQLPMy8xJ85nJ7inRaYiUn0kGaoRSqQK2QGe-BFfXJGq_HhEJb1SWUJt_eY6YCIDQp3HVn62Uh0tH9aaod7hdClEjLHAdEGOQy4cU6G67lvpUH1AY2BBX0xO5EAVLq3jo2lyJC9S0f3Mqn3k8jJpqxBvciAjl1Dauj5EO0-l7w-33qBKOOu_I6JxHa_nR0EW2eG5fwQCGRiTCJaBCexto1D7IjWFML39NsTkwaaj8okTQQizU9NFGVXHyIATZ-PPoJn8%3D)
__
---
On that day, I remember thinking this is it – _this is what it looks like when the Radical Left and their Deep State take political persecution and LAWFARE to its limit._
---
**_But boy was I wrong!_**
---
The Democrats would continue to go to unimaginable lengths to not just try to put me behind bars, but to utterly ruin me personally, professionally, and financially... all because I defended my dear friend, a true patriot, and our President – Donald J. Trump.
---
They proceeded to **STRIP me of my law licenses** and the means to defend myself. They forced me to the point of **BANKRUPTCY**. And even now, they’re trying to use bogus _state-level_ charges that President Trump cannot _federally_ pardon to throw me behind bars for the rest of my life!
---
But as I recently emailed to tell you, I still have hope that justice can prevail.
---
_Especially after this breaking report:_
---
_**“Republicans are demanding _FULL_ _RESTITUTION_ for Rudy Giuliani, who was charged with 13 felony counts and fined $148 million after contesting Georgia’s 2020 election, following Fulton County’s admission that 315,000 votes were fraudulently counted.”**_
---
My day for TRUE justice and restitution will come. If our free Republic is to survive, __it__ __has__ __to__.
---
Remember, this kind of political persecution won’t end with me. If we don’t FIGHT BACK here and now, then the precedent is set.
---
But I’m counting on every last patriot to stand with me and demand it!
---
And as it stands, I’m still missing _YOUR_ signature.
---
**So, I _humbly_ ask that YOU join the Republicans who are calling for “FULL RESTITUTION” for yours truly.**
---
This is the only way to make me whole, Peter.
---

* * *
# [Unpacking the U.S.-Israel Strikes On Iran](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=189560575&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg5NTYwNTc1LCJpYXQiOjE3NzIzODI4NTMsImV4cCI6MTc3NDk3NDg1MywiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.VO3DuMyTZGnw5fhpWuzSXlW6nI0Bw9i5nHVF57kKH6Y)
### In violating the Constitution, Donald Trump is playing a high-risk gamble with no clear endgame.
| | [Patrick J. Murphy](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---
| Mar 1
---
| [](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=189560575&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg5NTYwNTc1LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc3MjM4Mjg1MywiZXhwIjoxNzc0OTc0ODUzLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNDM5OTEzMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.JK11JIlHm4BomFdOc6-cdvSDv_J1hXY2DrXVYEed3zM&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=189560575&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg5NTYwNTc1LCJpYXQiOjE3NzIzODI4NTMsImV4cCI6MTc3NDk3NDg1MywiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.VO3DuMyTZGnw5fhpWuzSXlW6nI0Bw9i5nHVF57kKH6Y&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=189560575&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg5NTYwNTc1LCJpYXQiOjE3NzIzODI4NTMsImV4cCI6MTc3NDk3NDg1MywiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.VO3DuMyTZGnw5fhpWuzSXlW6nI0Bw9i5nHVF57kKH6Y)

| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/patrickmurphypa/p/unpacking-the-us-israel-strikes-on?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

At 2:30 in the morning on Saturday, Donald Trump posted an 8-minute video on Truth Social announcing that the United States had begun "major combat operations in Iran." By the time most Americans woke up, the Middle East was already on fire.
Iran retaliated, striking U.S. military installations across the Arabian Peninsula, including the U.S. Navy's Fifth Fleet headquarters in [Bahrain](https://substack.com/redirect/578dcfc9-ed7e-4a7e-b92c-d887f74a005e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), Al Udeid Air Base in [Qatar](https://substack.com/redirect/eff338dd-decd-45c8-8175-11e6781bcb54?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) -- the forward headquarters of U.S. Central Command -- Al Dhafra Air Base in [Abu Dhabi](https://substack.com/redirect/cb72c2e0-25f7-4c4a-a8f6-f2da62b02a5a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and U.S. Army Central in [Kuwait](https://substack.com/redirect/0b14ce80-c7be-46f6-b16d-0e844302787d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Israel and Jordan were also hit, and explosions were heard in [Riyadh](https://substack.com/redirect/f04c2cb5-1b75-4986-971e-723ddda88bf3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
The Secretary's SitRep is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..DZQpfUBHiNcI6_9BtArjMg9ATp89a2zDoLJqjSAPlZk?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=189560575)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/092e491e-830f-4949-9b26-9cb8b47b333e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
[Most strikes were intercepted](https://substack.com/redirect/bca469fa-d2d2-449d-9109-05acc4dcf15c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), but the entire Gulf is now inside this conflict.
To understand the scale of what's been mobilized, the United States has deployed 35,000 to 40,000 troops to the region -- the largest military buildup in two decades.
It was confirmed today that [3 U.S. Troops were killed](https://substack.com/redirect/8b776c6a-2056-41e0-a7ef-fd1c20fff7f1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the fighting with Iran, the first U.S. combat deaths of this conflict.
The joint US-Israel operation hit Iran's ballistic missile program, nuclear facilities, military sites, and ultimately took out Supreme Leader Khamenei, who led the country in this role for 36 years. Israel says its opening strikes decimated up to 40 targets in [Iran's chain of command](https://substack.com/redirect/5030af8e-8e52-4af2-82bf-ebb61234d84f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Among those confirmed killed: Khamenei's top security adviser, the commander of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, the Defense Minister, Khamenei's chief military secretary, the head of Iranian military intelligence, and the chairman of Iran's nuclear weapons research organization. Seven senior defense and intelligence officials are dead, with 30 top military and civilian leaders targeted overall.
That might seem like a win, but there are several challenges with Trump and Netanyahu's operation. For one, there is no organized political opposition waiting in Iran. Reza Pahlavi, the former Shah's son living in the U.S., has offered himself as a transitional leader -- Trump called him a "nice guy" but stopped short of an endorsement. That is not a plan.
The bigger challenge will be dealing with the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), which, in many ways, _is_ the regime. It has its own economic empire, its own political networks, and its own regional proxy relationships that operate semi-independently. Killing Khamenei and the top brass doesn't dissolve 125,000 personnel, billions in economic assets, and decades of entrenched relationships with Hamas, Hezbollah, and the Houthis overnight.
By all measures, this is a different animal than what happened last June, when first Israel and then the U.S. struck Iran's nuclear facilities in what became known as the 12-day war.
This is also the second time in a matter of a couple of months that this administration has launched a military operation aimed at removing a foreign government. Trump ordered the capture of Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro in January. Now Iran. The pattern of unilateral executive action with no congressional authorization is concerning. That's not a foreign policy doctrine we should normalize.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..DZQpfUBHiNcI6_9BtArjMg9ATp89a2zDoLJqjSAPlZk?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=189560575)
Important to note, the attack came despite U.S. intelligence assessments that Iran posed no immediate threat to the American mainland. The IAEA had found no evidence that Iran restarted its uranium enrichment program after Israel decimated it last year. And as recently as Thursday, U.S. and Iranian negotiators were making real progress in Geneva, with Oman's foreign minister brokering talks both sides described as promising.
Personally, I won't lose sleep over IRGC leaders being brought to justice. The IRGC supplied the roadside bombs that killed the majority of American troops in Iraq, including men and women I served alongside. Their fingerprints are on decades of Shia-led aggression across the Middle East. That accounting was long overdue.
But process still matters. When the U.S. moved against Maduro in Venezuela, Congress wasn't notified at all. This time, seven of the eight members of Congress's Gang of Eight were briefed last Tuesday by Secretary of State Marco Rubio -- a step in the right direction, even if it falls well short of the constitutional threshold the moment demands.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/0f94e696-133d-4d04-a5e6-05fa43819edd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
_On Friday, I spoke about the threat of looming war with Iran on MS NOW._
## **The Iranian People Deserve Better**
Before anything else, it's worth saying plainly: the Iranian regime is brutal, and not just to its own people. The IRGC is the world's leading state sponsor of terrorism, a regional bully that has spent decades exporting violence across the Middle East to maintain its grip on power. Since December, protesters have flooded the streets demanding an end to 47 years of clerical rule. They've faced a crackdown that, by credible estimates, has left more than 30,000 dead.
It's also worth remembering that Iran is not a monolithic country. While 90% are Shia Muslims, 60 percent of its population are ethnic minorities -- Azerbaijanis, Kurds, Arabs, Baluchis -- many of whom have their own grievances against Tehran that predate this regime. What comes after the ayatollahs matters enormously to communities that have been marginalized for decades.
The Iranian people deserve better than the regime that has held them down since 1979. They deserve the world's support. But it shouldn't come at the expense of violating the Constitution. Many tough guy keyboard warriors will read this and think it's some procedural TPS report, but it's literally the blueprint of our democracy - and the document we in the military take a blood oath to support and defend against all enemies foreign and domestic. To see leaders in the executive and legislative branches disregard it callously for over a year is infuriating and historically tragic.
Invoking the suffering of the Iranian people does not automatically justify unauthorized attacks, nor does it make Trump's method sound. You can believe that the ayatollahs must go and still ask hard questions about whether this is how you get there and what comes next.
My thoughts are with every American service member in the region right now, and with the diplomats and embassy staff serving across the Middle East who woke up yesterday inside a war zone they didn't start.
## **The Constitution Isn 't Optional**
Here is what I cannot let pass: the President of the United States launched what he himself called "major combat operations" against a sovereign nation without a single vote in Congress.
When President George W. Bush took the country to war in Iraq in 2003, he came to Congress first. There was a process. Senators and members of the House cast votes and were accountable for them. The Constitution's war-making power was, at minimum, acknowledged.
Trump did none of that. He announced a war on Truth Social.
Only [21% of Americans support the U.S. attacking Iran.](https://substack.com/redirect/6d346956-977d-4640-a474-addd10b11358?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

In 2003, [72% of Americans supported the war in Iraq.](https://substack.com/redirect/18381e30-bc14-4617-ab3d-f0091cae2d44?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The president does not have the unilateral authority to launch major combat operations against a nation we are not at war with. [Article I of the Constitution](https://substack.com/redirect/f867c081-1bfb-4d72-b5b7-740216e28c39?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) gives Congress the power to declare war.
You can support strong action against Iran's nuclear program and still believe that the Constitution requires more than a video posted to social media. These are not contradictory positions. A president who runs on ending "forever wars" and then launches one without a congressional vote is eroding the norms of executive power.
Trump is leaning into gray area defined in the War Powers Resolution of 1973, which gives the president some runway -- he can initiate military action, has 48 hours to notify Congress, and then has 60 days before he needs authorization to continue. Short-term strikes claimed under executive self-defense authority fall within what presidents of both parties have done for decades. Obama controversially exceeded this in 2011, when he launched a seven-month air campaign over Libya -- helping topple Muammar Gaddafi -- without congressional authorization. Trump also ordered an unauthorized strike that killed Iranian General Qasem Soleimani in 2020.
But the deeper concern isn't just this moment, it's the precedent. If a president can launch major combat operations against Iran without a congressional vote, what's the limiting principle? Venezuela was six weeks ago. Iran earlier this year and again this weekend. What's next? Cuba? At some point, the erosion of norms stops being a process and becomes a destination. And once those guardrails are gone, they are very hard to rebuild.
Trump spent his campaign promising to be the peacemaker the world needed. He invoked it constantly: the great dealmaker, the man who could end wars no one else could, the president who would bring peace to the Middle East. The one who would end the Russian invasion of Ukraine on day one. He is not wrong that something needed to change, but his method, especially here with Iran, is not peacemaking. It's a gamble.
If Trump's goal is truly a denuclearized Iran and a more stable Middle East, there was another path. He chose not to take it. The _[New York Times](https://substack.com/redirect/76607c0b-c8bb-40e0-9829-4bec3be02ec4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[ editorial board](https://substack.com/redirect/76607c0b-c8bb-40e0-9829-4bec3be02ec4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) put it bluntly this week: Trump's approach to Iran is "reckless," his goals "ill-defined," and he has "disregarded both domestic and international law for warfare."
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/c5960321-a99e-4262-9e3f-68594becd685?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
## **There Was Another Path**
We had a deal with Iran that ended their uranium enrichment in 2015 under President Obama. The Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action (JCPOA), known as the Iran nuclear deal, capped Iran's uranium enrichment, opened its facilities to IAEA inspectors, and in exchange, lifted economic sanctions on the regime. But the JCPOA was not ratified by the U.S. Congress. President Barack Obama did not submit it as a treaty, which would have required a two-thirds Senate majority; instead, it was treated as a political commitment or executive agreement.
While it wasn't a perfect agreement, diplomacy started cracking in 2018 when Trump withdrew from the JCPOA, reimplemented economic sanctions, with Iran responding by ramping uranium enrichment to 60% -- well above the 3.67% limit the deal had imposed. By January 2025, the IAEA formally declared Iran non-compliant. Every step away from the deal was a step toward this moment.
And yet, a new nuclear deal modeled on the 2015 JCPOA was being brokered in Geneva by the US and Iran. It would have unlocked [$141 billion in frozen Iranian assets](https://substack.com/redirect/ba82019d-c89c-47c6-b6b7-6630044231f8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), doubled oil exports within a year, and --according to researchers at Brandeis University -- added between [$600 billion and $1 trillion](https://substack.com/redirect/e33076b3-4388-4c79-b74d-17828a7653fb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to Iran's GDP by 2040. That's the economic case for diplomacy, and it was[ sitting on the table in Geneva as recently as Thursday](https://substack.com/redirect/a3da8ae3-19a2-477c-bdea-59ba53d06bd9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), where Oman's foreign minister described the talks as showing "significant progress" with another round already scheduled for Vienna next week.
## **Lessons From Past Middle East Revolutions**
We've seen this before, in recent memory, and each time the lesson was hard. The Arab Spring in December 2010 looked, for a moment, like the most hopeful thing to happen in the Middle East in decades. Ordinary people in Tunisia, Egypt, Libya, and Syria took to the streets, demanding dignity and democracy. The movements were characterized by protests against corruption, lack of economic opportunities, and dictatorial rule. The world cheered at first. Then we watched those revolutions get swallowed by military coups, civil wars, and in Syria's case, one of the worst humanitarian catastrophes of the 21st century. The old order was bad. What replaced it was often worse.
I say this as someone who served in Baghdad: the noble fight for freedom is incredibly fragile, and just because a situation is bad doesn't mean it can't get worse. Military success doesn't mean there will be a political solution.
The specter of Iraq looms over this.
Before the Iraq war became viewed as a failure, it was viewed as an immediate success.
Just six weeks after the invasion, Saddam Hussein's Sunni Muslim Ba'ath Party had been defanged. 300,000 Iraqi soldiers found themselves without a job. President Bush landed on the USS Abraham Lincoln, standing beneath a banner that read "Mission Accomplished," and declared major combat operations over.
The hard part, everyone assumed, was over. Then the insurgency started. The sectarian violence escalated. And then the war lasted another eight years, cost nearly [4,500 American lives](https://substack.com/redirect/92f98cfc-b785-4bd1-9883-7b84eee9c2b1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) \- including 19 of my brothers in our unit - and hundreds of thousands of Iraqi lives and left behind a country that two decades later is still struggling to hold itself together. Removing Saddam was the easy part. The hard part was everything after, and the Iraqi people are still paying for that failure.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..DZQpfUBHiNcI6_9BtArjMg9ATp89a2zDoLJqjSAPlZk?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=189560575)
## **How This Affects Russia-Ukraine**
The ripple effects extend beyond the Middle East.
Russia was quick to condemn the strikes as an "unprovoked act of armed aggression against a sovereign state" -- rich, coming from a country four years into its own invasion of Ukraine that's killed 500,000 Ukrainians and terrorized their people. But the relationship between Moscow and Tehran is worth understanding. Iran and Russia signed a Comprehensive Strategic Partnership in 2025, deepening cooperation on politics, security, trade, and technology.
Now, with Washington consumed by a new conflict in the Middle East, Putin has every incentive to harden his negotiating posture on Ukraine, drag out any peace talks, and play up American aggression to consolidate non-Western opinion around Moscow. Trump came into office promising to broker a Ukraine deal. A war with Iran gives Russia more leverage and Washington less bandwidth. Whether Putin uses this moment to complicate those talks or extract concessions is a question that should be on everyone's mind.
## **What the Markets Are Telling Us**
The financial world is bracing for Monday. Analysts were up all night running scenarios, and their consensus is that this is not a normal geopolitical flare-up.
All eyes are on the Strait of Hormuz. About [20 million barrels of crude oil](https://substack.com/redirect/ac1c4700-97fb-4a72-92a6-61251fc7e802?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) per day flow through that narrow passage between Iran and Oman -- roughly[ 20-25% ](https://substack.com/redirect/ac1c4700-97fb-4a72-92a6-61251fc7e802?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)of the world's seaborne crude. Iran's navy and missile forces sit on one side of it. If Iran makes any serious move to close or threaten the strait, oil prices will spike violently, and the ripple effects on everything from gas prices to global inflation to equity markets will be severe.
Economists are [projecting](https://substack.com/redirect/21643e23-07a2-43bc-8839-9a774e2f5f19?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) global equities could open Monday down 1-2% or more, U.S. Treasury yields falling 5-10 basis points as money moves into safe havens, and oil jumping 5-10%. Gold and the dollar are expected to strengthen as investors move away from risk. These projections assume the conflict stays contained. If it doesn't -- if this becomes a weeks or months-long campaign aimed at regime change, with sustained Iranian retaliation across the region -- markets will react far more badly.
Asian markets are particularly exposed. The region's economies depend heavily on stable energy supplies and open sea lanes. Extended conflict would hurt them in ways that a short, contained operation would not.
No one in finance knows how long this lasts. That uncertainty alone is its own market event.
## **What Could Still Come Out of This**
If these unconstitutional and unprovoked attacks somehow lead to a real transition in Iran, a post-regime government could normalize relations with the region, and the long-term consequences would be profound. That's the unilateral gamble that's being taken here.
The Abraham Accords, brokered during Trump's first term, were a major economic and diplomatic breakthrough. They brought Israel and several Arab states into formal diplomatic and economic relationships that would have seemed impossible a decade earlier. Sunni-led Saudi Arabia was next in line before October 7, 2023 derailed everything. That massacre was launched by Hamas, an Iranian proxy, killing 1200 Israeli civilians and holding over 250 hostage. A post-theocratic Iran willing to live alongside its neighbors rather than fund their enemies would change the entire regional dynamic. It could potentially complete what the Abraham Accords started, pulling the whole region toward integration.
But that outcome requires a plan for what comes after the bombs. It requires a legitimate successor to the regime with real support on the ground. It requires the kind of sustained, serious post-conflict commitment that the United States has repeatedly shown it lacks the patience for. Hope is not a strategy - and a president who won't ask Congress that's under Republican control for a war authorization is unlikely to ask Congress for a 20-year reconstruction budget.
The Iranian people deserve a better future. What happens in the next few days, weeks, and months will determine whether this brutal weekend was the beginning of something better or just the beginning of something worse.
## **A Prayer for Peace**
In the meantime, especially now during the season of Lent, Ramadan, and Purim, let's pray for peace and wisdom for our political leaders.
Over 40,000 Americans are deployed now in the Middle East. They are doing what they are trained to do. While they may be the best in the world, their families are worried sick here at home watching the news as everyone is on alert or sheltered in place.
Over 90 million Iranian civilians don't know what happens next.
***
_Dear God - We lift up the mothers and fathers who are packing bags, standing at gates, watching buses pull away and memorizing the sound of their child 's voice._
_For the parents whose sons and daughters are deploying right now - steady their hearts. Calm the quiet places where fear tries to take root. Replace helplessness with trust and anxiety with courage._
_Guard their children in body, mind, and spirit._
_For the parents just beginning this journey - who have watched their child raise a right hand and swear an oath - prepare them for what they cannot yet understand._
_Let pride be stronger than fear._
_Let purpose steady the unknown._
_Remind them that raising someone willing to serve is not loss - it is legacy._
_Surround every military family with community._
_Let no parent sit alone in silent worry. Prompt us to call one another, to check in, to show up. Make us generous with meals, with time, with listening, with prayer._
_Protect those who deploy._
_Strengthen those who wait._
_Carry the young men and women who step into harm 's way. Let them feel the prayers of home in their loneliest hours._
_And in a time of loud opinions and instant judgments, teach us grace and restraint._
_Help those with strong convictions to take pause_
_and remember that behind every headline is a son or daughter, and behind every uniform is a family holding its breath._
_Grant wisdom to speak with care. Grant humility to listen before reacting. Grant all of us the grace to separate policy debates from personal sacrifice._
_Let us honor the courage of those who serve even when we disagree about the world they walk into._
_Protect our unity._
_Protect our families._
_Protect our peace._
_Bind us together in courage, humility and love._

In violating the Constitution, Donald Trump is playing a high-risk gamble with no clear endgame. | | | Mar 1 | | | | | | | | | | | | At 2:30 in the morning on Saturday, Donald Trump posted an 8-minute video on Truth Social announcing that the United States had begun "major combat operations in Iran." By the time most Americans woke up, the Middle East was already on fire. Iran retaliated, striking U.S. military installations across the Arabian Peninsula, including the U.S. Navy's Fifth Fleet headquarters in , Al Udeid Air Base in -- the forward headquarters of U.S. Central Command -- Al Dhafra Air Base in , and U.S. Army Central in . Israel and Jordan were also hit, and explosions were heard in . The Secretary's SitRep is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. | | ---|---|--- , but the entire Gulf is now inside this conflict. To understand the scale of what's been mobilized, the United States has deployed 35,000 to 40,000 troops to the region -- the largest military buildup in two decades. It was confirmed today that in the fighting with Iran, the first U.S. combat deaths of this conflict. The joint US-Israel operation hit Iran's ballistic missile program, nuclear facilities, military sites, and ultimately took out Supreme Leader Khamenei, who led the country in this role for 36 years. Israel says its opening strikes decimated up to 40 targets in . Among those confirmed killed: Khamenei's top security adviser, the commander of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, the Defense Minister, Khamenei's chief military secretary, the head of Iranian military intelligence, and the chairman of Iran's nuclear weapons research organization. Seven senior defense and intelligence officials are dead, with 30 top military and civilian leaders targeted overall. That might seem like a win, but there are several challenges with Trump and Netanyahu's operation. For one, there is no organized political opposition waiting in Iran. Reza Pahlavi, the former Shah's son living in the U.S., has offered himself as a transitional leader -- Trump called him a "nice guy" but stopped short of an endorsement. That is not a plan. The bigger challenge will be dealing with the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), which, in many ways, is the regime. It has its own economic empire, its own political networks, and its own regional proxy relationships that operate semi-independently. Killing Khamenei and the top brass doesn't dissolve 125,000 personnel, billions in economic assets, and decades of entrenched relationships with Hamas, Hezbollah, and the Houthis overnight. By all measures, this is a different animal than what happened last June, when first Israel and then the U.S. struck Iran's nuclear facilities in what became known as the 12-day war. This is also the second time in a matter of a couple of months that this administration has launched a military operation aimed at removing a foreign government. Trump ordered the capture of Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro in January. Now Iran. The pattern of unilateral executive action with no congressional authorization is concerning. That's not a foreign policy doctrine we should normalize. Important to note, the attack came despite U.S. intelligence assessments that Iran posed no immediate threat to the American mainland. The IAEA had found no evidence that Iran restarted its uranium enrichment program after Israel decimated it last year. And as recently as Thursday, U.S. and Iranian negotiators were making real progress in Geneva, with Oman's foreign minister brokering talks both sides described as promising. Personally, I won't lose sleep over IRGC leaders being brought to justice. The IRGC supplied the roadside bombs that killed the majority of American troops in Iraq, including men and women I served alongside. Their fingerprints are on decades of Shia-led aggression across the Middle East. That accounting was long overdue. But process still matters. When the U.S. moved against Maduro in Venezuela, Congress wasn't notified at all. This time, seven of the eight members of Congress's Gang of Eight were briefed last Tuesday by Secretary of State Marco Rubio -- a step in the right direction, even if it falls well short of the constitutional threshold the moment demands. On Friday, I spoke about the threat of looming war with Iran on MS NOW. The Iranian People Deserve Better Before anything else, it's worth saying plainly: the Iranian regime is brutal, and not just to its own people. The IRGC is the world's leading state sponsor of terrorism, a regional bully that has spent decades exporting violence across the Middle East to maintain its grip on power. Since December, protesters have flooded the streets demanding an end to 47 years of clerical rule. They've faced a crackdown that, by credible estimates, has left more than 30,000 dead. It's also worth remembering that Iran is not a monolithic country. While 90% are Shia Muslims, 60 percent of its population are ethnic minorities -- Azerbaijanis, Kurds, Arabs, Baluchis -- many of whom have their own grievances against Tehran that predate this regime. What comes after the ayatollahs matters enormously to communities that have been marginalized for decades. The Iranian people deserve better than the regime that has held them down since 1979. They deserve the world's support. But it shouldn't come at the expense of violating the Constitution. Many tough guy keyboard warriors will read this and think it's some procedural TPS report, but it's literally the blueprint of our democracy - and the document we in the military take a blood oath to support and defend against all enemies foreign and domestic. To see leaders in the executive and legislative branches disregard it callously for over a year is infuriating and historically tragic. Invoking the suffering of the Iranian people does not automatically justify unauthorized attacks, nor does it make Trump's method sound. You can believe that the ayatollahs must go and still ask hard questions about whether this is how you get there and what comes next. My thoughts are with every American service member in the region right now, and with the diplomats and embassy staff serving across the Middle East who woke up yesterday inside a war zone they didn't start. The Constitution Isn 't Optional Here is what I cannot let pass: the President of the United States launched what he himself called "major combat operations" against a sovereign nation without a single vote in Congress. When President George W. Bush took the country to war in Iraq in 2003, he came to Congress first. There was a process. Senators and members of the House cast votes and were accountable for them. The Constitution's war-making power was, at minimum, acknowledged. Trump did none of that. He announced a war on Truth Social. Only In 2003, The president does not have the unilateral authority to launch major combat operations against a nation we are not at war with. gives Congress the power to declare war. You can support strong action against Iran's nuclear program and still believe that the Constitution requires more than a video posted to social media. These are not contradictory positions. A president who runs on ending "forever wars" and then launches one without a congressional vote is eroding the norms of executive power. Trump is leaning into gray area defined in the War Powers Resolution of 1973, which gives the president some runway -- he can initiate military action, has 48 hours to notify Congress, and then has 60 days before he needs authorization to continue. Short-term strikes claimed under executive self-defense authority fall within what presidents of both parties have done for decades. Obama controversially exceeded this in 2011, when he launched a seven-month air campaign over Libya -- helping topple Muammar Gaddafi -- without congressional authorization. Trump also ordered an unauthorized strike that killed Iranian General Qasem Soleimani in 2020. But the deeper concern isn't just this moment, it's the precedent. If a president can launch major combat operations against Iran without a congressional vote, what's the limiting principle? Venezuela was six weeks ago. Iran earlier this year and again this weekend. What's next? Cuba? At some point, the erosion of norms stops being a process and becomes a destination. And once those guardrails are gone, they are very hard to rebuild. Trump spent his campaign promising to be the peacemaker the world needed. He invoked it constantly: the great dealmaker, the man who could end wars no one else could, the president who would bring peace to the Middle East. The one who would end the Russian invasion of Ukraine on day one. He is not wrong that something needed to change, but his method, especially here with Iran, is not peacemaking. It's a gamble. If Trump's goal is truly a denuclearized Iran and a more stable Middle East, there was another path. He chose not to take it. The put it bluntly this week: Trump's approach to Iran is "reckless," his goals "ill-defined," and he has "disregarded both domestic and international law for warfare." | | ---|---|--- There Was Another Path We had a deal with Iran that ended their uranium enrichment in 2015 under President Obama. The Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action (JCPOA), known as the Iran nuclear deal, capped Iran's uranium enrichment, opened its facilities to IAEA inspectors, and in exchange, lifted economic sanctions on the regime. But the JCPOA was not ratified by the U.S. Congress. President Barack Obama did not submit it as a treaty, which would have required a two-thirds Senate majority; instead, it was treated as a political commitment or executive agreement. While it wasn't a perfect agreement, diplomacy started cracking in 2018 when Trump withdrew from the JCPOA, reimplemented economic sanctions, with Iran responding by ramping uranium enrichment to 60% -- well above the 3.67% limit the deal had imposed. By January 2025, the IAEA formally declared Iran non-compliant. Every step away from the deal was a step toward this moment. And yet, a new nuclear deal modeled on the 2015 JCPOA was being brokered in Geneva by the US and Iran. It would have unlocked , doubled oil exports within a year, and --according to researchers at Brandeis University -- added between to Iran's GDP by 2040. That's the economic case for diplomacy, and it was, where Oman's foreign minister described the talks as showing "significant progress" with another round already scheduled for Vienna next week. Lessons From Past Middle East Revolutions We've seen this before, in recent memory, and each time the lesson was hard. The Arab Spring in December 2010 looked, for a moment, like the most hopeful thing to happen in the Middle East in decades. Then the insurgency started. The sectarian violence escalated. And then the war lasted another eight years, cost nearly \- including 19 of my brothers in our unit - and hundreds of thousands of Iraqi lives and left behind a country that two decades later is still struggling to hold itself together. Removing Saddam was the easy part. The hard part was everything after, and the Iraqi people are still paying for that failure. How This Affects Russia-Ukraine The ripple effects extend beyond the Middle East. Russia was quick to condemn the strikes as an "unprovoked act of armed aggression against a sovereign state" -- rich, coming from a country four years into its own invasion of Ukraine that's killed 500,000 Ukrainians and terrorized their people. Trump came into office promising to broker a Ukraine deal. A war with Iran gives Russia more leverage and Washington less bandwidth. Whether Putin uses this moment to complicate those talks or extract concessions is a question that should be on everyone's mind. You're reading the March edition of **_The JanePAC Monthly_** — and in a news cycle full of chaos, we have some updates worth celebrating. From bold legislation in New Jersey to a landmark victory for solar in Virginia, our climate champions are delivering. Plus: a Supreme Court showdown you need to know about and a Jane Fonda quote worth wearing. Let's dive in. 👇
* * *
**🏆 JANEPAC CLIMATE CHAMPIONS IN ACTION** | **Our New Jersey Champions Are Standing Up to ICE**
---
JanePAC-endorsed Assemblymembers _Katie Brennan_ and _Ravi Bhalla_ introduced the **F** ight **U** nlawful **C** onduct and **K** eep **I** ndividuals and **C** ommunities **E** mpowered Act.
Yes, the acronym spells exactly what you think it does.
The F*** ICE Act would give people the power to sue in state court when their constitutional rights are violated through federal immigration enforcement. And it’s only one piece of a broader legislative push to hold ICE accountable in New Jersey.
Katie and Ravi are putting real weight behind their words and driving this fight forward. This is exactly the kind of bold, unapologetic leadership JanePAC fights to put in office.
**[CLICK HERE TO LEARN MORE ABOUT NJ'S FIGHT TO HOLD ICE ACCOUNTABLE →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/126417969/604856622/1566603232?refcode=ea.13ee4ce3-8b15-f111-a69a-000d3a57593f&ms=ea.13ee4ce3-8b15-f111-a69a-000d3a57593f&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjEzZWU0Y2UzLThiMTUtZjExMS1hNjlhLTAwMGQzYTU3NTkzZiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=zol8GideMuSmrfqkTmnvuGbrzyEpdSM3MRQXQpzu6Ro=&emci=3404ca92-1a14-f111-a69a-000d3a57593f&emdi=13ee4ce3-8b15-f111-a69a-000d3a57593f&ceid=4823105)**
**Virginia’s Climate Majority is Already Delivering**
---
Last November, JanePAC-endorsed candidates won every single race we invested in across Virginia — giving Democrats their largest majority in the House of Delegates in three decades. Now, that majority is delivering results.
The Virginia House and Senate have both passed legislation that would prohibit local governments from banning large solar projects, while establishing sensible development standards to address community concerns.
Similar bills failed in previous years. This time, with our expanded majorities and a Democratic trifecta in Richmond, it passed. None of this is possible without the grassroots movement that helped put these champions in office.
[**READ THE FULL STORY →**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/126417970/604856623/556138608?refcode=ea.13ee4ce3-8b15-f111-a69a-000d3a57593f&ms=ea.13ee4ce3-8b15-f111-a69a-000d3a57593f&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjEzZWU0Y2UzLThiMTUtZjExMS1hNjlhLTAwMGQzYTU3NTkzZiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=zol8GideMuSmrfqkTmnvuGbrzyEpdSM3MRQXQpzu6Ro=&emci=3404ca92-1a14-f111-a69a-000d3a57593f&emdi=13ee4ce3-8b15-f111-a69a-000d3a57593f&ceid=4823105)

* * *
**⚖️ SUPREME COURT CLIMATE SHOWDOWN**
The Supreme Court agreed to hear a case that could determine whether cities and municipalities across America can hold Big Oil legally accountable for the costs of climate change — and the stakes could not be higher.
**“The court will hear an appeal brought by Suncor and Exxon Mobil asking it to throw out a lawsuit in Colorado seeking damages for harm caused by the use of fossil fuels.”**

_**\- NBC NEWS**_
---
ExxonMobil and Suncor Energy are appealing a Colorado Supreme Court ruling that allowed Boulder's landmark climate lawsuit to move forward in state court. If the Supreme Court sides with them, it could shut down similar lawsuits filed by communities across the country seeking billions in damages for extreme temperatures, wildfires, and other climate harms.
The Trump administration has already filed a brief siding with Big Oil. The outcome of this case will shape the future of climate accountability for decades to come.
[**READ THE FULL STORY →**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/126417973/604856626/865025576?refcode=ea.13ee4ce3-8b15-f111-a69a-000d3a57593f&ms=ea.13ee4ce3-8b15-f111-a69a-000d3a57593f&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjEzZWU0Y2UzLThiMTUtZjExMS1hNjlhLTAwMGQzYTU3NTkzZiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=zol8GideMuSmrfqkTmnvuGbrzyEpdSM3MRQXQpzu6Ro=&emci=3404ca92-1a14-f111-a69a-000d3a57593f&emdi=13ee4ce3-8b15-f111-a69a-000d3a57593f&ceid=4823105)

**📖 WEEKEND READ**
_The Guardian_ recently spotlighted a jaw-dropping interview with author Beth Gardiner about her new book Plastic Inc: Big Oil, Big Money and the Plan to Trash our Future — and it is a must-read.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/126417974/604856627/-591015872?igsh=MXV0Zm8zc3o4NzBl&refcode=ea.13ee4ce3-8b15-f111-a69a-000d3a57593f&ms=ea.13ee4ce3-8b15-f111-a69a-000d3a57593f&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjEzZWU0Y2UzLThiMTUtZjExMS1hNjlhLTAwMGQzYTU3NTkzZiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=zol8GideMuSmrfqkTmnvuGbrzyEpdSM3MRQXQpzu6Ro=&emci=3404ca92-1a14-f111-a69a-000d3a57593f&emdi=13ee4ce3-8b15-f111-a69a-000d3a57593f&ceid=4823105)
Gardiner reveals how the oil and plastic industries deliberately pushed plastic into every corner of our lives, then spent decades shifting the blame for the resulting crisis onto consumers.
**From the founding of Keep America Beautiful in the 1950s to the myth of recycling, it's a story of calculated deception in pursuit of profit.**
[**READ MORE HERE →**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/126417975/604856628/-591015872?igsh=MXV0Zm8zc3o4NzBl&refcode=ea.13ee4ce3-8b15-f111-a69a-000d3a57593f&ms=ea.13ee4ce3-8b15-f111-a69a-000d3a57593f&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjEzZWU0Y2UzLThiMTUtZjExMS1hNjlhLTAwMGQzYTU3NTkzZiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=zol8GideMuSmrfqkTmnvuGbrzyEpdSM3MRQXQpzu6Ro=&emci=3404ca92-1a14-f111-a69a-000d3a57593f&emdi=13ee4ce3-8b15-f111-a69a-000d3a57593f&ceid=4823105)

clean_body: Another month of updates you don't want to miss. | You're reading the March edition of The JanePAC Monthly — and in a news cycle full of chaos, we have some updates worth celebrating. From bold legislation in New Jersey to a landmark victory for solar in Virginia, our climate champions are delivering. Plus: a Supreme Court showdown you need to know about and a Jane Fonda quote worth wearing. Let's dive in. 👇 🏆 JANEPAC CLIMATE CHAMPIONS IN ACTION | Our New Jersey Champions Are Standing Up to ICE JanePAC-endorsed Assemblymembers Katie Brennan and Ravi Bhalla introduced the F ight U nlawful C onduct and K eep I ndividuals and C ommunities E mpowered Act. Yes, the acronym spells exactly what you think it does. The F ICE Act would give people the power to sue in state court when their constitutional rights are violated through federal immigration enforcement. And it’s only one piece of a broader legislative push to hold ICE accountable in New Jersey. Katie and Ravi are putting real weight behind their words and driving this fight forward. This is exactly the kind of bold, unapologetic leadership JanePAC fights to put in office. Virginia’s Climate Majority is Already Delivering Last November, JanePAC-endorsed candidates won every single race we invested in across Virginia — giving Democrats their largest majority in the House of Delegates in three decades. Now, that majority is delivering results. The Virginia House and Senate have both passed legislation that would prohibit local governments from banning large solar projects, while establishing sensible development standards to address community concerns. Similar bills failed in previous years. This time, with our expanded majorities and a Democratic trifecta in Richmond, it passed. None of this is possible without the grassroots movement that helped put these champions in office. 📰 WHERE'S JANE? February marked one year since Jane took the stage at the Screen Actors Guild Awards and delivered a line that's only grown more powerful since: In a political climate where authoritarians wield "woke" as a slur, Jane turned it into a rallying cry. And now you can wear it with pride! ⚖️ SUPREME COURT CLIMATE SHOWDOWN The Supreme Court agreed to hear a case that could determine whether cities and municipalities across America can hold Big Oil legally accountable for the costs of climate change — and the stakes could not be higher. “The court will hear an appeal brought by Suncor and Exxon Mobil asking it to throw out a lawsuit in Colorado seeking damages for harm caused by the use of fossil fuels.” \- NBC NEWS ExxonMobil and Suncor Energy are appealing a Colorado Supreme Court ruling that allowed Boulder's landmark climate lawsuit to move forward in state court. If the Supreme Court sides with them, it could shut down similar lawsuits filed by communities across the country seeking billions in damages for extreme temperatures, wildfires, and other climate harms. The Trump administration has already filed a brief siding with Big Oil. The outcome of this case will shape the future of climate accountability for decades to come. 📖 WEEKEND READ The Guardian recently spotlighted a jaw-dropping interview with author Beth Gardiner about her new book Plastic Inc: Big Oil, Big Money and the Plan to Trash our Future — and it is a must-read. Gardiner reveals how the oil and plastic industries deliberately pushed plastic into every corner of our lives, then spent decades shifting the blame for the resulting crisis onto consumers. From the founding of Keep America Beautiful in the 1950s to the myth of recycling, it's a story of calculated deception in pursuit of profit. That's it for this edition of The JanePAC Monthly. Thank you for being part of this movement. If you want to stay plugged in between newsletters, follow us on social media for daily updates and behind-the-scenes moments with Jane and the team: Together, we are building a movement they cannot ignore. And we're just getting started. In solidarity, – Team JanePAC = | = | = = Follow along: | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|--- Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee. Send donations via check to: Jane Fonda Climate PAC 600 Pennsylvania Ave SE #15180 Washington, DC 20003 United States

| Friend,
Accountability for our leaders is crucial for a functioning democracy. Without it, our democracy cannot survive.
On Wednesday, Trump Attorney General Pam Bondi showed that MAGA has zero regard for accountability.
Bondi appeared before the House Judiciary Committee to answer questions about the Trump administration's handling of the Epstein files and escalating ICE violence in American cities.
Instead of providing answers, she delivered partisan attacks, MAGA talking points, and open contempt for the representatives questioning her on behalf of the American people.
She even refused to acknowledge the victims of the horrific abuse at the hands of the Epstein class and how Bondi's own DOJ revictimized them when they redacted the names of powerful men, but NOT their victims.
---
| [](https://zzejj9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001fNKpEAYG1kjWuiML4oDXRr91PpRTo8k7Atsb0892pfeXXKh8bdwK6--xMPMVNsDKcznuVQVhYzQg-IQ6G2q8EA-pDwddw-adYdZ8GR7LrlvOhf-LV3H34mvSMk3n4xnKmZnaY-VvQepdzLTUOn1F4XwRtXD8hXDXBQQvNHmvyFvqbJcr_rT1qEAVnwI4c33PS3t0fpw5dcKSGb_dWiyYuPGtfs1pKFzlQnEBH5ormlWMyPs5fg3wPm4qWN4k5JosUNE_Nw7KnRA=&c=siurREFSQudYhYvhbDAUoziezbKPnC0-33kzTlC8WzcGjFnsgNL8Tw==&ch=VF7AR-DAm2yEEsmhpx8lziAYB8mUHgP9-H_JapEWNS1pWShyyr0KGg==)
---
| Bondi is covering up the indiscriminate murder of American citizens by ICE and the identities of the wealthy elite who committed heinous acts of pedophilia and violent sexual abuse alongside Epstein.
Bondi herself displayed the arrogance of someone who does not believe she will ever face accountability.
And that's because accountability is not part of MAGA's plan.
It was Bondi's FBI that seized election records in Georgia. It is Bondi who attempted to extort voter records from Minnesota. It was Bondi's FBI who summoned state election officials to a secret meeting last week.
Pam Bondi is central to the MAGA plan to steal elections, and she knows that if they succeed, none of them will EVER be held to account. That is why Defend Our Courts exists.
If federal leaders refuse to enforce the rule of law, state courts, and ultimately state Supreme Courts, become the last line of defense for the most fundamental right of all Americans: the right to vote.
In 2025, with support from more than 100,000 grassroots donors, Defend Our Courts helped defend the pro-democracy majority on Pennsylvania's Supreme Court, protecting a critical electoral firewall in one of the country's most important swing states.
Now we are scaling that model nationwide to defend, flip, and build state Supreme Court majorities before the next election cycle. But we cannot do it without YOU.
[If you believe leaders must be held accountable and elections must remain free and fair, please step up today. Can we count on you to rush a contribution of $25, $15, or whatever is meaningful to you to help defend the courts that defend our democracy?](https://zzejj9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001fNKpEAYG1kjWuiML4oDXRr91PpRTo8k7Atsb0892pfeXXKh8bdwK6--xMPMVNsDKcznuVQVhYzQg-IQ6G2q8EA-pDwddw-adYdZ8GR7LrlvOhf-LV3H34mvSMk3n4xnKmZnaY-VvQepdzLTUOn1F4XwRtXD8hXDXBQQvNHmvyFvqbJcr_rT1qEAVnwI4c33PS3t0fpw5dcKSGb_dWiyYuPGtfs1pKFzlQnEBH5ormlWMyPs5fg3wPtmfFvIY8BNvK9iObdJNKlk=&c=siurREFSQudYhYvhbDAUoziezbKPnC0-33kzTlC8WzcGjFnsgNL8Tw==&ch=VF7AR-DAm2yEEsmhpx8lziAYB8mUHgP9-H_JapEWNS1pWShyyr0KGg==)
