It wasn't easy telling the world I grew up dirt poor, I was raised by my Mamaw while my mother struggled with addiction.
It wasn't exactly the prep school/trust fund/summer-in-the-Hamptons upbringing that is commonplace in DC.
But I felt God called me to tell my story. Warts and all. I felt led to speak out because I believe millions of Americans share stories like mine. Stories of jobs being outsourced to China, addiction ravaging communities and killing our neighbors, and heeding the call of duty after the Towers fell on 9/11.
As your Vice President, I now stand in the breach for the left-behind, forgotten men and women who built this country, but were abandoned by the political class in DC.
I'll do everything in my power to make sure that never happens again. I'll fight every day for good folks like you.
I promise to always stand between you and the Democrat Machine that aims to obliterate everything that makes our country great.
[Which brings me to the purpose of my email today.](http://links.conservativeum.com/s/c/QvrOWoNzQZQl2eX07iZ9xrEX3lMOwJcMFVixXjwRygEx_kwbUK9pS3oLpBolfkcGLXhgzSAgHqsBCT6vOkcIGC9RnilM43w1XmNvJhL7JvccXGcpfBWLShfTC_K1W28MxRAMvmNFsV9BsaFRH1I-23C8eFhjHOIVvTmhFi0_5CyPknK_oCRJ9FoXs5nVXKAUJopWt9cX6P6QR4yNDyAMFJE_Is2MsfyeADeMtiWml-7Xj1UdhXtuMteob_kJJUQVL1sqGtlfZ9ILylCyPI2ezmjHPkSbI8XNS1uPJogOOXDLRn-dJaawzy8j8xlbO2sVNo2NHWwi3GSEw3fpcFUAsFes-6hDpDoVx1cuKXqombo3lf1LOFW0S4XPATsyl06PvfAFI4dyCJ3O_9YaH1uiioBnsb-hHjGUsusqM3I3_3CnvlU-Y2fI4Kk_9GyO57HJRbenEmxzU9ylfv51b9LhqKY_T8h0UzxokwnGwTPRBrfEZOJVbOGDm3RJ8WRhMYbMndcFSoTjwy-D06JraRGciiGU8Zd934-XlmG3asITVbz9SlCNdp40z_wx9-oldNKSURk-2da21v57-j03S4pvPgGpU-pP94joUSWKoiAQQo1igP-XFhsrjZZX0z7MqTXDPa9TI1dAR7RJpokgW2OkNr7p_2jqNuRVGmh5cdFbqBe_n9sAFZ_7qeFm2okzRayv9-fEfqPGHXghOsKH5x2nANzBZyTtQmgl38bdQ1_Xg4Df1lpVxTsXgH-X6Yyqgd3jryu9GJMl5s_wxshxpuKL3RwaxrXDjcV7zpmSpi-UGCV0OpljZR5GzVc/slyx_7a_rcR1wSLijCYl2_235SExDEdj/18)

So now we're forced to rebuild.
The Democrat Machine is ALREADY crushing us in fundraising, ALREADY outspending us in key races, and ALREADY building the campaign infrastructure to decimate us in the Midterm Elections.
At this point, the consequences of simply resting on our laurels would be unthinkable.

clean_body: fellow Americans - it is Vice President JD Vance. It wasn't easy telling the world I grew up dirt poor, I was raised by my Mamaw while my mother struggled with addiction. It wasn't exactly the prep school/trust fund/summer-in-the-Hamptons upbringing that is commonplace in DC. But I felt God called me to tell my story. Warts and all. I felt led to speak out because I believe millions of Americans share stories like mine. Stories of jobs being outsourced to China, addiction ravaging communities and killing our neighbors, and heeding the call of duty after the Towers fell on 9/11. As your Vice President, I now stand in the breach for the left-behind, forgotten men and women who built this country, but were abandoned by the political class in DC. I'll do everything in my power to make sure that never happens again. I'll fight every day for good folks like you. I promise to always stand between you and the Democrat Machine that aims to obliterate everything that makes our country great. We're coming out of the most expensive election in American history. I mean that. Kamala Harris spent over a billion dollars against us. It took EVERY PENNY we had to get our story out there, expose her ultra-Liberal record, reach undecided voters, and win. So now we're forced to rebuild. The Democrat Machine is ALREADY crushing us in fundraising, ALREADY outspending us in key races, and ALREADY building the campaign infrastructure to decimate us in the Midterm Elections. At this point, the consequences of simply resting on our laurels would be unthinkable. So here's my final pitch to you: I understand that's ambitious. But if that's what it takes to deliver President Trump a Republican House for his entire second term, it's a no-brainer. I'll do what it takes to win. Do you think you can help us get there? Whether it's $5 or $500 - the amount you give isn't nearly as important as how many folks step up: , you've probably heard bits and pieces of my story at this point. If my humble beginnings taught me anything, it's to never give up, never surrender, and never go down without a fight. That's why I'm partnering with Steve Scalise to help lay the critical groundwork we need to win. Regardless, it's an honor to fight alongside you. God bless America, Vice President JD Vance * + top Conservative leaders at the heart of our MAGA Movement have reached out with GRAVE WARNINGS on the state of the Midterms like NEVER BEFORE... Democrat controlled House would be the worst-case scenario for the MAGA Movement. We're pleading with you to take this seriously. | | | Paid for by TEAM SCALISE, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and to benefit SCALISE FOR CONGRESS and EYE OF THE TIGER PAC. We have some news to share, friend: **We just fell $1,382 short of our critical end-of-month fundraising goal.**
We know that doesn’t seem like a lot but every single dollar and every single deadline counts in a race like ours — _especially because Fox News recently called it a top pickup opportunity for the GOP_ and betting markets show this race narrowing. Our opponents are seizing on it as a green light to pour even more money into New Mexico. So please:

| | [Donate](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/4677?refcode=jtk288-fr-nd&t=3&akid=7797%2E400544%2Efl9XrH)

But with an already crowded Republican field, bolstered by multi-millionaire, self-funding candidates and significant outside spending, it's going to take **all of us working together, hitting every fundraising goal, to keep New Mexico blue and elect a leader who will fight fiercely for New Mexicans.**

name: Marina Jenkins, NDRC
subject: The Supreme Court is weighing an existential decision
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President
body:
Here’s what you can do to defend fair representation in this critical moment.
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/126419209/604861125/-3547583?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImIyY2I2NDE1LTkwMTUtZjExMS1hNjlhLTAwMGQzYTU3NTkzZiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=tKTp3Y5eENyIT7CXMZBuU0udzut_B2sk5RdE06RNJek=&emci=d1c7ff3c-0014-f111-a69a-000d3a57593f&emdi=b2cb6415-9015-f111-a69a-000d3a57593f&ceid=8431045)
---

Peter, the fate of the Voting Rights Act is at stake right now. The Supreme Court’s decision in _Louisiana v. Callais_ — which could come down any day now — weighs the constitutionality of a critical protection against racial discrimination. The ruling could be groundshifting in several ways:
1. **The Supreme Court could uphold Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act,** which prohibits states from engaging in election practices that have the effect of discriminating against people of color.
2. **The Supreme Court could decide that Louisiana’s map needs to be redrawn,** adding another redistricting fight to happen ahead of the midterm elections.
3. **The Supreme Court could strike down all or part of the crown jewel of the civil rights movement** — and immediately make our national redistricting crisis a whole lot worse, potentially giving cover to several states to start new mid-decade redraws.

The only thing we know with certainty is that the NDRC must be ready to spring into action as soon as this ruling comes down. **We cannot risk being caught flat-footed on a ruling that could dramatically change representative democracy in America.** [**Make a gift today to ensure that our rapid-response plan is ready for the critical months ahead.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/126419210/604861126/-1096702678?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_MarchSCOTUSPreview_email_20260301-NDRC-Tier1-MarchSCOTUSPreview_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20260301-NDRC-Tier1-MarchSCOTUSPreview_&utm_campaign=MarchSCOTUSPreview&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImIyY2I2NDE1LTkwMTUtZjExMS1hNjlhLTAwMGQzYTU3NTkzZiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=tKTp3Y5eENyIT7CXMZBuU0udzut_B2sk5RdE06RNJek=) | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

clean_body: Here’s what you can do to defend fair representation in this critical moment. | Peter, the fate of the Voting Rights Act is at stake right now. The Supreme Court’s decision in Louisiana v. Callais — which could come down any day now — weighs the constitutionality of a critical protection against racial discrimination. The ruling could be groundshifting in several ways: 1. The Supreme Court could uphold Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act, which prohibits states from engaging in election practices that have the effect of discriminating against people of color. 2. The Supreme Court could decide that Louisiana’s map needs to be redrawn, adding another redistricting fight to happen ahead of the midterm elections. 3. The Supreme Court could strike down all or part of the crown jewel of the civil rights movement — and immediately make our national redistricting crisis a whole lot worse, potentially giving cover to several states to start new mid-decade redraws. The only thing we know with certainty is that the NDRC must be ready to spring into action as soon as this ruling comes down. We cannot risk being caught flat-footed on a ruling that could dramatically change representative democracy in America. = | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = If the Supreme Court weakens the essential protection that is the VRA, the decision will open the floodgates for more redistricting chaos. Republican state lawmakers and anti-democracy forces are eager to use this ruling as an excuse to push mid-decade redraws in their own states. We must be equipped with the resources to push back against the extreme anti-democracy efforts that could follow this Supreme Court ruling. The NDRC was built for this moment to defend the fair representation of millions. = Thank you for ensuring that we can take on anything, Marina Jenkins Executive Director National Democratic Redistricting Committee = = | | Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President. | | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|---|--- National Democratic Redistricting Committee 712 H St NE, #25 Washington, DC 20002 United States if you'd like to receive fewer emails from the NDRC. Text MAPS to 36787, STOP to end. Msg and Data rates may apply

name: Justin Streiff, Turning Point USA
subject: What our students are up against this spring...
body:
They’re trying to intimidate a generation| | | [](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=c6ab0919f3e64a129868966c7a28638d&_e=lpmG7rIhbgzA8h0R3XLlyepwRsiLj2wbuMjP6v3xRtK3IGlQ4aXlvdzgTrQa5SzjnpWT7JLjNKYWIsETYL8Av0QaTmtnGfQ0X-Olw1nVMHSTHf0WPmwnlBg4omtlUKpZ1Ets3QTfttDZbr_kgmGal8wY6aPHW6Dsj79_XW3rLuxK1wYh_GGKFV5Xpp_9iKLltL9j76OKSOeIMno84wWtiZ-2-4KhY-z5gPAhzCbSBamx21MN4PMQSf1NmpM5eGwlglsRKrYDCiW63AcpdNGXR43f8dpAQrJHd6VA5WIV4Qn3GkwRH43GcMEOmA5ssqEIt5LKy7SiyRVqn4tkiSXucPxMZsmgjafRgATB0wwK1KmNCpxdlt4GFwEMvO1Xs7rvpz7cUOack6-ekUW1nxbCwmd1p4ZjsYHFdIQ8K5sqiTSMsBuH1rRvMrhCshM0-_DL2XkhVPwG1ZOTGkZGWHRPo5un7lv_uGumEsaFSiDCpPHf9Zo6gcQx8-7QkMKURErB7QPXpL-l7CxinKzuMCdHEchamKrzxwu0Fz5rVyfIeJCBLgtvogW3tBkH0C132B_1sxNq7pK1q02SEuX6qnfyyOD95Frgdyd5HdSNByvjp9ZBsZL8MuDiH5Ya1b3eebO30y_QnlkRFWgzWJxiavmrVuHd3pfieXy0PCb8M5OZZgbVQqle_PX4Aup_8AMld-qeu4rDP5jZrAtiz4A0dPu5oSM_bOTA8YhNOhV8qrd-Gu8%3D)
---
| | Patriot,
---
I have to say it...

The campus climate this spring?

_It 's been downright ugly._

To be frank, the Left has never made it easy for our students. That's always come with the territory when you're speaking truth deep in the belly of the beast...

But this semester is different.

**This has been one of the most hostile semesters Turning Point USA students have ever faced. **

From coast to coast, high school and college students who dared to stand for faith, freedom, and this great nation have been stalked, slandered, threatened, and in some cases -- _physically attacked. _

All for saying what you and I know to be true:

That America is worth defending. That speech must remain free. And that Charlie Kirk's life and legacy meant something.

And despite the opposition -- our students didn't back down.

They didn't run. They didn't hide.

They showed up -- earlier, louder, and stronger than ever before.

_But not without cost. _

**Let me tell you more about what our students have been up against so far this year... **

In Maryland, one of our high school leaders -- just 17 years old -- helped organize a Club America event on campus back in December.

It was peaceful. Students only. Parental permission required. No outside adults. Everything done by the book.

And yet, weeks later, during a recent local school board meeting...

A local woman stood up and publicly accused this student of "grooming children."

Then she went even further.

She told the board: _" All members of this Board are mandatory reporters... Based on the circumstances surrounding this event, **a report was made to Child Protective Services. " **_

Yes, you read that right -- she weaponized CPS against one of our students.

Filed against a **minor. **

For organizing a pro-America Turning Point USA event with his friends.

Now, I want you to imagine being 17 years old... doing everything right... following every rule... showing up with courage and conviction...

And then hearing a stranger stand up in front of your entire community and imply that you're a danger to children.

That you need to be investigated. That you're the problem.
---
[](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=c6ab0919f3e64a129868966c7a28638d&_e=lpmG7rIhbgzA8h0R3XLlyepwRsiLj2wbuMjP6v3xRtK3IGlQ4aXlvdzgTrQa5SzjnpWT7JLjNKYWIsETYL8Av0QaTmtnGfQ0X-Olw1nVMHSTHf0WPmwnlBg4omtlUKpZ1Ets3QTfttDZbr_kgmGal8wY6aPHW6Dsj79_XW3rLuxK1wYh_GGKFV5Xpp_9iKLltL9j76OKSOeIMno84wWtiZ-2-4KhY-z5gPAhzCbSBamx21MN4PMQSf1NmpM5eGwlU8RYjbSCupBcIKuK-llSFEGmJQsx51SzFTyAEKAdpZbamNbCNfcXmtHbFf2Yzl2QLKEDHTjqdfHE5UTmZb3HFnwE6PlB7TY07txHSssbKeehqTYvx31UhJqwdjP0pY7OzvXUB7YC9LWBI01tGIbQhCbXEaDcK43ipi1UQWgvNGTWwEyYV6nxaNspKkOeb7elFyPs_sbJAEaEii2qD7si4K7cGo40SSgBU3gvXg6tWWE3zObGrEzyjJP00n238zwgwG6slrh4Vpfm_AdxbkrC9AbTTr8FiYPkfvh_a-GReT7JDr2ME6L4y-Mny-dBc21LLzNvFiiOQUf5JuC7yhMHE7NcjAkokYTdNT_Wcdlr5iEwyNMx_s4vR015IP8yDmgPCBDl6cqSjmTO-PbVp37RCvIKC6o-B4ZhJS3JwEizupEpohDDsjzwnUJWSfw5aXg9YFRRqQdWnAXX31TmOH5HHeRnf9fVTbR0oZcuj7rUDiI%3D)
---
_**Above:** "Nancy" -- the Leftist Karen who filed a CPS report against a local Club America chapter and spoke out against them at a recent school board meeting. _
---
How would _you_ respond?

Because I'll tell you what he did...

He walked up to that microphone -- calm, steady, unshaken -- and said:

_" We have been accused of many things... I'm the president of the group. I'm 17, I'm a minor. I can't groom children because I am a child." _

Seventeen years old. Standing alone. But standing strong in front of his entire community while facing outrageous, malicious accusations.

That's courage. The kind of courage Charlie would've been proud of.

And that courage is catching fire across the country. _But so is the hatred. _

Because what happened in Maryland wasn't an isolated incident... It's just the beginning:

🚨 **At Lawrence University in Wisconsin, Leftists on campus are trying to permanently shut down a brand-new TPUSA chapter before it even starts. **

Never mind that Lawrence has over 160 student clubs... and not one is right-leaning. Or that the university handbook bans discrimination based on political affiliation.

One of our students there is now receiving death threats.

And instead of protecting him?

The Left-wing student government is putting the chapter's fate up to a campus-wide vote.

🚨 **At Iowa State University, **Colby Brandt -- the student body president -- was threatened with _**impeachment**_ because he had connections to TPUSA. Fortunately, his "impeachment" was dismissed by the student government's judicial branch... But this was only after the outrage that followed on social media and the university's administration came to its senses.
---
[](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=c6ab0919f3e64a129868966c7a28638d&_e=lpmG7rIhbgzA8h0R3XLlyepwRsiLj2wbuMjP6v3xRtK3IGlQ4aXlvdzgTrQa5SzjnpWT7JLjNKYWIsETYL8Av0QaTmtnGfQ0X-Olw1nVMHSTHf0WPmwnlBg4omtlUKpZ1Ets3QTfttDZbr_kgmGal8wY6aPHW6Dsj79_XW3rLuxK1wYh_GGKFV5Xpp_9iKLltL9j76OKSOeIMno84wWtiZ-2-4KhY-z5gPAhzCbSBamx21MN4PMQSf1NmpM5eGwl-OVeSrAUNgA5hgBr3K5vyxceapsXyqOKfANZcNLBZNYBuKvBqkrX7LlZCbcdvh6djA4_6qBh8KgakaqmN4_97EFFYIqTpcA2kHBADOzMoheOozkniFdVHaEmn41kucN59MoiGLlbb5dMqPm5vbMt8TOr4qb2qO2sWPs34T8HkAIsZ2rsK6Osolxf9uT0Elk2eVFyoRs82PFHO8IqGpWNi8FAUuOb5TpnQN69v5kEWrJJITXqn0lN5njraatjzpAmi3DlKW6Gtyz09zTJrzDkdPbLRmEYgZoR1OZIRvdTvlIRbLXdrCDbl7e6wSO5YCNQe1ZsNE5EDpNIO7Ey1phnAN0D3xlgMIqJQGsV_09xxmMGT2PYBCYO00FFCnWHQxBwfeROAJ0XpENJRIG-Z2tZVoRWZ2EjQycyx2I8_1D7vkgHYCfyvdmPALJw8P_HsLYviUl00FAzMbYbTKGamRgmmXkVkFz6VhSPGihgyMxlNmc%3D)
---
🚨 **At Worcester Polytechnic,** one of our signs that featured a quote with Charlie was vandalized with the phrase: "he should've ducked a**hole."

🚨 **At Lake Zurich High School,** one of our students held an "I ❤️ ICE" sign -- and was physically threatened by a peer. On school grounds and caught on camera.
---
[](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=c6ab0919f3e64a129868966c7a28638d&_e=lpmG7rIhbgzA8h0R3XLlyepwRsiLj2wbuMjP6v3xRtK3IGlQ4aXlvdzgTrQa5SzjnpWT7JLjNKYWIsETYL8Av0QaTmtnGfQ0X-Olw1nVMHSTHf0WPmwnlBg4omtlUKpZ1Ets3QTfttDZbr_kgmGal8wY6aPHW6Dsj79_XW3rLuxK1wYh_GGKFV5Xpp_9iKLltL9j76OKSOeIMno84wWtiZ-2-4KhY-z5gPAhzCbSBamx21MN4PMQSf1NmpM5eGwlQ49Owy32M2hxjKqhMuPbJcTKDA_GvkivoodU89RqtSRAVthEYbU3S88vymHySa4ELX1TcSIswqcxg4t-udexxFbJ8WnAyxfNjeZJ60z4EGowFOWv5XMO-bwm_m0OR7y2Q6zmEhPTRufJBlXwFP9fZ7eIm_X43zKN5Wia42XJi9y1usQ7LYISl2teLXg-3a28efPXMbPrlJBmJwJvMEvoJt7NhGBBWUPPhqC-maW-h_HAfTCZiE0gmjPY4LbThoazOxish_s4dpe8uAHrHMtcmBvcMR0MnKAQmePhvq05RkrIiBTCVZUW9tYeIB6rc2T_TcEqeEC9aODGb_TwfMK2R1JvvKy5Awv1683xv7TllYZvhDiwO2k5IThEAv2wPt0OPVJ1wTeJ6WackwUZn_t3kNNtO08sB0eqD0wOiQp2BEcG8mp7x1UNy2oqb9SYLL-ODFnEh7iO23u1IAtkS483pKJCiJ-VET_aEfshQ30etgA%3D)
---
🚨 **At the University of Houston, **a Leftist mocked Charlie Kirk's assassination while harassing TPUSA students at a tabling event on campus -- wishing cancer and paralysis on their families while making sexual gestures.

🚨 **And at Coastal Carolina Community College,** a professor was recorded saying this in front of his class:

_"Did he (Charlie Kirk) deserve to die? No... but..."_ Then he added: _" He was a racist piece of s**t. And Turning Point USA is a racist piece of s**t organization." _

The message was clear.
---
[](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=c6ab0919f3e64a129868966c7a28638d&_e=lpmG7rIhbgzA8h0R3XLlyepwRsiLj2wbuMjP6v3xRtK3IGlQ4aXlvdzgTrQa5SzjnpWT7JLjNKYWIsETYL8Av0QaTmtnGfQ0X-Olw1nVMHSTHf0WPmwnlBg4omtlUKpZ1Ets3QTfttDZbr_kgmGal8wY6aPHW6Dsj79_XW3rLuxK1wYh_GGKFV5Xpp_9iKLltL9j76OKSOeIMno84wWtiZ-2-4KhY-z5gPAhzCbSBamx21MN4PMQSf1NmpM5eGwlaHKo6UqmDwo7TvzO_uDqiVchf1RCMEBoglXfqg03IPEDkd5bZUjcfj5qhIQpqbcrEvauCVofDB-0tnFaPVhl17lxXmmLgov10nxUA_LGSwL7n2WBSTE4oSh99QqmiPoKx29sxOnKXaIxmIyYDY9Esu5o9JL0zzp6CMOOOoEHdxU0muDApME6yzZmF99XCtwKfcrRow_SA_Hy9KOyjOcTU__QGsO-8nam0HIuZdXZlj-SrDhE15gn194717R7CU-lPzoKFSxz4hUPMf68boAR564f_sxaJFsRNFwmNd_GDXxTmaJzU-AzuQp2CCbcZFMGCxrImu7RJMj1RbhW2vbxlzkYGt6JDPFUj4_0DHd2Gwx5N86vmoW_X6QW3ZuS_wz9YsR8qbbXFwUj2tmBQRNlysG0iVvUMOFD5gNaZZMyLRkRFGnCv4R_AMBWRFHQ-mBT4AxROsnB-O_MboQ9fV4QNxl7BVmoPPnGXdnXbme4xQI%3D)
---
I could go on and on with more examples of the Left's hatred towards our students...

But while they mock Charlie's death, encourage hate, harassment, and try to poison students against the very ideas our country was built on...

In the face of all this -- _our students aren 't folding. _

Not one bit.

They're still tabling at campus events.

They're still meeting after school -- still recruiting, still growing.

They're still passing out pocket Constitutions and American flags.

They're still launching chapters in hostile territory -- places where they've been told, outright, that they're not welcome.

**And while their peers stage "ICE Walkouts" and chaos in the halls... **

_**Our students are gathering to pray -- for their schools, for law enforcement, and for this nation -- quietly around their school's flagpole. **_
---
[](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=c6ab0919f3e64a129868966c7a28638d&_e=lpmG7rIhbgzA8h0R3XLlyepwRsiLj2wbuMjP6v3xRtK3IGlQ4aXlvdzgTrQa5SzjnpWT7JLjNKYWIsETYL8Av0QaTmtnGfQ0X-Olw1nVMHSTHf0WPmwnlBg4omtlUKpZ1Ets3QTfttDZbr_kgmGal8wY6aPHW6Dsj79_XW3rLuxK1wYh_GGKFV5Xpp_9iKLltL9j76OKSOeIMno84wWtiZ-2-4KhY-z5gPAhzCbSBamx21MN4PMQSf1NmpM5eGwlkD7mygDbiJf4MVysNBDmo1Ohk9kvUlu2M4FdxWxtzT9S3Vd_PVGBPFC6ygdcm96bOWwFyjZdsh6hQWvTl7J8VqJsUFZzSUCxOl1vouWiXTDXfniwH-5LTVhR_RmXbjvj7Acbwgsrpa5Lzf5pqBrWZr-CTq69mnvaDhRbRxUu3NSfC1Jgg1vgsjjcRKo6NYPphW_mVOxtQ9oda7kAnssEKXVYGNuhRrY0EFTXWFRmGHU6aMDFQnvn4dtRFKbeZQdo8-2q4uPuWuMFRj7SbL8VssIP6ARRpd4koBgffG_KOiroPHmF55y7PkQbsonUvHfiECvnRDNtYDpPep6vh0bUkI8bTRpahkOeOXKicQqL4EzFkD4CNb4X-l90Al-xcU1OsU-Ws1T-CFboUwNBLggtaafUagSXUByeZIDNYyFtzYdu2ZBfBL7CirLiZRVR5263RZKVBbS3aRk_zIApi_qnPeh3X1QucOx1B9cnA0xNNPE%3D)
---
That's because these students made a decision -- the same decision Charlie challenged them to every day: _" It doesn't take skill to be courageous. It takes a choice." _

They chose courage.

They chose to stand for faith, freedom, and family. They chose to defend everything that makes this country worth fighting for.

They truly are the courageous generation.

**In fact, since Charlie 's assassination last September: **

clean_body: They’re trying to intimidate a generation| | | | | Patriot, I have to say it... The campus climate this spring? It 's been downright ugly. To be frank, the Left has never made it easy for our students. That's always come with the territory when you're speaking truth deep in the belly of the beast... But this semester is different. This has been one of the most hostile semesters Turning Point USA students have ever faced. From coast to coast, high school and college students who dared to stand for faith, freedom, and this great nation have been stalked, slandered, threatened, and in some cases -- physically attacked. All for saying what you and I know to be true: That America is worth defending. That speech must remain free. And that Charlie Kirk's life and legacy meant something. And despite the opposition -- our students didn't back down. They didn't run. They didn't hide. They showed up -- earlier, louder, and stronger than ever before. But not without cost. Let me tell you more about what our students have been up against so far this year... In Maryland, one of our high school leaders -- just 17 years old -- helped organize a Club America event on campus back in December. It was peaceful. Students only. Parental permission required. No outside adults. Everything done by the book. And yet, weeks later, during a recent local school board meeting... A local woman stood up and publicly accused this student of "grooming children." Then she went even further. She told the board: " All members of this Board are mandatory reporters... Based on the circumstances surrounding this event, a report was made to Child Protective Services. " Yes, you read that right -- she weaponized CPS against one of our students. Filed against a minor. For organizing a pro-America Turning Point USA event with his friends. Now, I want you to imagine being 17 years old... doing everything right... following every rule... showing up with courage and conviction... And then hearing a stranger stand up in front of your entire community and imply that you're a danger to children. That you need to be investigated. That you're the problem. Above: "Nancy" -- the Leftist Karen who filed a CPS report against a local Club America chapter and spoke out against them at a recent school board meeting. How would you respond? Because I'll tell you what he did... He walked up to that microphone -- calm, steady, unshaken -- and said: " We have been accused of many things... I'm the president of the group. I'm 17, I'm a minor. I can't groom children because I am a child." Seventeen years old. Standing alone. But standing strong in front of his entire community while facing outrageous, malicious accusations. That's courage. The kind of courage Charlie would've been proud of. And that courage is catching fire across the country. But so is the hatred. Because what happened in Maryland wasn't an isolated incident... It's just the beginning: 🚨 At Lawrence University in Wisconsin, Leftists on campus are trying to permanently shut down a brand-new TPUSA chapter before it even starts. Never mind that Lawrence has over 160 student clubs... and not one is right-leaning. Or that the university handbook bans discrimination based on political affiliation. One of our students there is now receiving death threats. And instead of protecting him? The Left-wing student government is putting the chapter's fate up to a campus-wide vote. 🚨 At Iowa State University, Colby Brandt -- the student body president -- was threatened with impeachment because he had connections to TPUSA. Fortunately, his "impeachment" was dismissed by the student government's judicial branch... But this was only after the outrage that followed on social media and the university's administration came to its senses. 🚨 At Worcester Polytechnic, one of our signs that featured a quote with Charlie was vandalized with the phrase: "he should've ducked ahole." 🚨 At Lake Zurich High School, one of our students held an "I ❤️ ICE" sign -- and was physically threatened by a peer. On school grounds and caught on camera. 🚨 At the University of Houston, a Leftist mocked Charlie Kirk's assassination while harassing TPUSA students at a tabling event on campus -- wishing cancer and paralysis on their families while making sexual gestures. 🚨 And at Coastal Carolina Community College, a professor was recorded saying this in front of his class: "Did he (Charlie Kirk) deserve to die? No... but..." Then he added: " He was a racist piece of st. And Turning Point USA is a racist piece of st organization." The message was clear. I could go on and on with more examples of the Left's hatred towards our students... But while they mock Charlie's death, encourage hate, harassment, and try to poison students against the very ideas our country was built on... In the face of all this -- our students aren 't folding. Not one bit. They're still tabling at campus events. They're still meeting after school -- still recruiting, still growing. They're still passing out pocket Constitutions and American flags. They're still launching chapters in hostile territory -- places where they've been told, outright, that they're not welcome. And while their peers stage "ICE Walkouts" and chaos in the halls... Our students are gathering to pray -- for their schools, for law enforcement, and for this nation -- quietly around their school's flagpole. That's because these students made a decision -- the same decision Charlie challenged them to every day: " It doesn't take skill to be courageous. It takes a choice." They chose courage. They chose to stand for faith, freedom, and family. They chose to defend everything that makes this country worth fighting for. They truly are the courageous generation. In fact, since Charlie 's assassination last September: 🔥 Over 140,000 students raised their hands to get involved 🔥 4,600+ active chapters are now operating across America 🔥 More than 1 million students are part of this movement And Patriot, patriots like you are the reason they've been able to do it... Because while the Left mocked Charlie's death-- while the hate followed and the attacks intensified -- you didn't disappear. Patriots like you stood firm. You stood with these students. You made it clear: The mission doesn 't end here. And now, because of your faithfulness, we 're watching the seeds Charlie planted come to life in high schools, colleges, and communities across this country. Our students may not know your name... But they know someone made it possible for them to take a stand on their campus. They're walking into classrooms bolder than ever. Speaking truth in places where lies once ruled. Because someone gave them the chance. That someone was you. Patriots like you reminded these students that they're not alone -- even when it feels like it. You helped push back the darkness. You made space for courage to grow. The kind of courage Charlie lived for. The kind of courage he gave everything for. So, thank you, Patriot. Thank you for giving these young patriots a fighting chance. We will not stop. We will not retreat. We will keep standing for truth -- louder and stronger than ever before. Because Charlie's mission didn't end with his life. It's being carried forward -- by thousands of students... and by you. And that 's the one thing the Left can't stand. Let's keep pushing. It's truly incredible watching these students from across the country lining up to start the next wave of new Turning Point chapters. Yes, I speak plainly. Yes, I say what I mean. That’s not chaos. That’s meeting people where they’re at. I’m not running to impress political consultants. I’m running to turn out the people who’ve been overlooked, underestimated, and told to wait their turn. Young voters. Black voters. Latino voters. Folks who stopped showing up because nobody showed up for them. Some people whisper about “electability.” But let me tell you something: you’re electable when people are excited enough to vote. My opponent has Super PACs spending millions against me. But we have something they can’t manufacture: energy. Authenticity. And a base that is waking up. = = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Texas doesn’t need safe. Texas needs a fighter. — Jasmine Crockett = = | | | = | For too long, Texas has elected Senators who have defended politics as usual and protected the status quo, while Texans pay the price. We’ve had Senators who’ve pushed the American Dream further and further out of reach. Jasmine is running for the United States Senate because she believes Texas deserves a Senator who will be an independent voice for all 30 million Texans – not a rubber stamp or party line vote for Donald Trump. = Email is an important way to stay in contact but if you feel like you’re receiving too much, click here to only receive the most important messages. ** PAID FOR BY JASMINE FOR TEXAS Jasmine for Texas P.O. Box 227235 Dallas, TX 75222 United States

Democrats are turning out in numbers that have not been seen in years. The energy is real. The urgency is real. And the stakes are real. | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/jaworskifortexas.com/last-night-14193095?e=5d8d80652b)

| | , **We are two days away from Primary Day.** **And what we are seeing across Texas is historic.** Democrats are turning out in numbers that have not been seen in years. The energy is real. The urgency is real. And the stakes are real. People are showing up because they understand that what happens in this primary matters. It determines who carries our banner into November. It determines who will defend the Constitution, protect voting rights, fight corruption, and stand up for working families. **Momentum is powerful, but only if each of us follows through.** If you have already voted, thank you. If you have not yet cast your ballot, I am asking you plainly and directly: make your plan today. [**Look up your polling place.**](https://jaworskifortexas.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=2e1f50319ad85f5f9e5c11ac9&id=25cbfdc1cf&e=5d8d80652b)**Set your time. Bring a friend. Show up.** For months I have traveled this state, meeting Texans face to face, answering hard questions, earning endorsements, and laying out exactly what I will do as your Attorney General. I have shown you who I am. **Now it is your turn.** Historic turnout is a signal. It tells us that Democrats are awake and ready. Let’s finish strong. Let’s make sure every vote is cast and every voice is heard. **Two days.** Onward to the polls,

**
**

name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: I’m getting back on the road
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Powered by People, [poweredxpeople
body:
# [](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/43706?t=1&akid=240722%2E444211%2E0hnhEp)
It’s time to say goodbye to my family and our dogs, cats, and chickens and hit the road.
I’m torn because I’ll miss everyone and I also recognize that when I’m gone Amy is doing all the parenting, all the doctor’s visits, all the checkups at the vet. And she’s been carrying that for a long time!
But I also know—and Amy knows too—that this work is so important. Our ability to meet young Texans where they are, everywhere across this state, is how we make sure they’re registered, they’re committed, and they’re voting in November.
Over this next week I’ll be working with our organizers and volunteers at Texas Southern University in Houston, University of Texas at Austin, Texas A&M; in College Station and then back to Harris County for a day at the University of Houston.
So far this year I’ve been to dozens of towns and campuses—from Prairie View A&M; in Waller County to Midwestern State University in Wichita County—and I see firsthand just what a difference our work makes.
[__](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20260301.XXTfpq&refcode=ema20260301-240722&t=3&akid=240722%2E444211%2E0hnhEp)
[__](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20260301.XXTfpq&refcode=ema20260301-240722&t=4&akid=240722%2E444211%2E0hnhEp)
[__](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20260301.XXTfpq&refcode=ema20260301-240722&t=5&akid=240722%2E444211%2E0hnhEp)
Students light up when someone takes the time to meet them, listen to their concerns, talk about elections and voting, and then support them as they register and prepare to vote in their first elections.
Volunteers light up too! They are able to feel the agency and power they have as they add new voters to the rolls and build personal relationships with the young Texans they’ve met that will allow them to help them vote come November.
[**Please support this work with a $3 contribution today. It keeps us on the road and ensures we can do the work.**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20260301.XXTfpq&refcode=ema20260301-240722&t=6&akid=240722%2E444211%2E0hnhEp)
**[Donate $3](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20260301.XXTfpq&refcode=ema20260301-240722&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=25&akid=240722%2E444211%2E0hnhEp)**
On behalf of all of our organizers, our volunteers and the O’Rourke family—THANK YOU!
Beto

[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pbp-em-2023-footer?refcode=em20260301-240722-footer&t=26&refcode2=240722_444211_0hnhEp&akid=240722%2E444211%2E0hnhEp)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/update-contact-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=27&akid=240722%2E444211%2E0hnhEp)

Paid for by Powered by People, [poweredxpeople.org](http://www.poweredxpeople.org?akid=240722%2E444211%2E0hnhEp), not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

name: David Hogg
subject: We’re not writing Florida off
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
If we’re serious about fighting for our future, we have to expand the battlefield.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20260301-1&t=1&akid=3201%2E587540%2EUrTiQ7)
Peter,
For years, Democrats have looked at Florida and just shrugged, writing it off completely. They say it’s a lost cause, that there’s no way we can gain ground in that kind of political landscape. Too red, too expensive, too far gone.
But listen: I have good reason to believe otherwise. I recently spent several days in Florida meeting with prospective candidates and connecting with organizers, and what I saw was potential. Real, tangible potential.
Florida is red because the infrastructure to compete consistently isn’t there. Republicans invested for decades in school boards, county councils, state legislative seats, and judicial races. They built that power from the ground up.
**That’s not luck or destiny, that’s strategy. And strategy can be beaten.**
Is it complicated, messy, even? Yes. But that’s why it matters so much, and why the kind of organizing we’re building there isn’t just important. It’s urgent.
Florida has become a testing ground for some of the most extreme and heinous policies in the country. Book bans pushed through school districts, attacks on LGBTQ+ kids, six week abortion bans, and more recently, cooperation agreements that have expanded ICE’s footprint within local law enforcement, and sweeping immigration raids that have torn families apart and led to thousands of arrests across immigrant communities. These policies are reshaping people’s everyday lives.
**Even in the face of all that, Florida is not lost, Peter. Not for us, not for the people we’re fighting for, and definitely not for the movement we’re building. It’s a challenge, yes, but the best opportunities always are.**
If we’re serious about building durable power, we can’t just defend the bluest states and hope for the best, we have to compete _everywhere._
_Given that the Republican for governor is likely to be one of the weakest Republicans to run for governor in my lifetime, we’ve got a real shot at breaking the super majority and winning the governor's race for the first time in nearly a decade._
Florida is still a massive state with millions of persuadable voters. Rapidly changing suburbs, some of the largest youth populations in the country, and working class people who are tired of being used as political props instead of being heard. None of that disappears after a few brutal election cycles.
This is the reality: If we abandon states, or ignore them altogether when it gets hard, we shrink the map of opportunity and limit what’s possible in our future.
Like I said, the right didn’t win Florida overnight. They invested, and they organized.
So will we.
Because giving up is not an option. We don’t win by chasing the easiest ground. We win by expanding the playing field, and Florida has the potential to be one of the biggest pickup opportunities in the country if we’re willing to think long term.
**[I’m not giving up on the Sunshine State, and you shouldn’t either. Will you chip in $5 today to help us build lasting power in Florida and support the next generation of young, bold leaders in the state?](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20260301_1_florida.G_7AiD&refcode=em20260301-3201&amount=5&t=2&akid=3201%2E587540%2EUrTiQ7)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20260301_1_florida.G_7AiD&amount=5&refcode=em20260301-3201&t=21&akid=3201%2E587540%2EUrTiQ7)**
Thank you,
David Hogg
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-footer?refcode=em20260301-1&t=22&refcode2=3201_587540_UrTiQ7&akid=3201%2E587540%2EUrTiQ7)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:

[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.wN2m2QjBCTHFUQ01rUYFVelMKayspAe0d_k0UEqrtLk?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=189563497)
* * *
_**by Eric Lullove, Blue Amp Media Medical Contributing Editor**_
The role of the U.S. Surgeon General has historically been the "nation's doctor"--a position built on the bedrock of rigorous clinical training, board certification, and an unwavering commitment to evidence-based public health. From C. Everett Koop to Luther Terry, this office has been occupied by individuals who, regardless of their political stripes, were fundamentally grounded in the reality of clinical medicine.
Watching the recent Senate HELP Committee confirmation hearing for Casey Means, I couldn't help but feel that we are witnessing the erosion of that standard.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5f76533d-50f8-426f-9a2f-916714b41359?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
### **The Credentials Gap**
The most alarming aspect of this nomination isn't just the political baggage--it's the fundamental lack of clinical readiness. Dr. Means, a graduate of Stanford Medical School, never completed a residency program and is not board-certified in any specialty. Her medical license is currently held in inactive status.
In the surgical and wound care world, we understand that experience is earned at the bedside and in the operating room. There is no substitute for the years of rigorous training required to earn board certification. When we discuss public health policy that affects millions, we aren't talking about "wellness coaching" or social media optimization; we are talking about life-and-death decisions, infectious disease protocols, and the translation of complex data for a lay population. Promoting an individual who walked away from clinical practice--and who has no residency training--to lead the U.S. Public Health Service Commissioned Corps is not just unconventional; it is a profound dereliction of the standards we should demand for the nation's health.
* * *
**Cliff 's Note:** _If you love fresh seafood, staying fit, and wanna help BAM grow --a trifecta!--please click on the link below and check out these delicious and health options. Thanks!_
* * *
###### SPONSORED
### **Why More Seafood (and Less Red Meat) Might Be the Wellness Shift You 've Been Looking For**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/3b9203cb-07bb-469d-b5d5-83f03142a110?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_**[If you're thinking about cutting back on red meat for better health, wild-caught seafood could be your secret weapon](https://substack.com/redirect/3b9203cb-07bb-469d-b5d5-83f03142a110?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). **Science shows that eating fish 1-2 times a week can support healthy heart and brain function, help reduce inflammation, and even may give your metabolism a little boost._
_Here 's what wild-caught seafood delivers: essential n‐3 polyunsaturated fats (specifically, the marine-based omega-3 fatty acids that nutritionists love so much!), lean and digestible protein, and a smorgasbord of nutrients. [Compare that to conventional red meat, which often comes with higher saturated fat, an unhealthy balance of omega‐6 fatty acids, and a limited range of nutrients](https://substack.com/redirect/371dd7ed-2449-425d-9297-269a3356b8f3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)._
_Switching to seafood as your go-to protein is not just a shift away from a red meat rut. It 's a shift toward higher-quality nutrients and a more diversified diet for well-rounded health._
[Upgrade Your Diet](https://substack.com/redirect/3b9203cb-07bb-469d-b5d5-83f03142a110?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
### **The Hearing Performance: A Failure to Clarify**
The Senate hearing this week was not just "tense"; it was a masterclass in deflection. When pressed by both Republican and Democratic senators on critical issues--most notably the routine childhood vaccine schedule and the efficacy of the Hepatitis B vaccine for newborns--Dr. Means repeatedly retreated into the nebulous language of "informed consent" and "shared decision-making."
While autonomy is a core tenet of medical ethics, the Surgeon General is not a private practice doctor discussing a single case with a patient; they are the chief spokesperson for public health. When a nominee for this role refuses to clearly affirm the safety and necessity of the routine vaccine schedule in the face of measles outbreaks, they aren't practicing "nuance"--they are signaling an alignment with anti-vaccine rhetoric that undermines the very institutions they would be tasked to lead.
Dr. Means attempted to frame her skepticism as a push for "gold standard science," yet she struggled to distance herself from the disinformation that has plagued federal agencies for years. Her inability to provide a clear, unequivocal endorsement of foundational public health measures--despite being "grilled" by the committee--was, in my view, a failure to demonstrate the clarity, resolve, and professional authority the office requires.
### **The Wellness "Influencer" Problem**
We are living in an era where "wellness influencers" often command more attention than peer-reviewed literature. Dr. Means' career--built on social media sponsorships, supplement promotion, and diet-centric messaging--is precisely the kind of commercialization of medicine that threatens to erode public trust.
If confirmed, the Surgeon General will hold a massive megaphone. If that megaphone is used to advance the "Make America Healthy Again" agenda through the lens of a non-practicing influencer--rather than a seasoned, board-certified physician committed to the established medical consensus--the implications for our national health infrastructure are severe.
We deserve a Surgeon General who leads with clinical, peer-reviewed evidence. We do not need an influencer whose primary background is in navigating the social media algorithm rather than the hospital wards.
The Senate has a constitutional, moral, ethical and legal responsibility to reject her confirmation.
_Dr. Lullove is a Board-Certified Podiatric Physician and Wound Care Specialist based in South Florida. His expertise in public health policy, reimbursement models, AI models for physicians as well as researcher, investigator and subject-matter expert accounts for the bulk of his career work._

Clinical Reality vs. Wellness Marketing: The Case for a Qualified Surgeon General. | | and | Mar 1 | | | ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- | | ---|---|--- Blue Amp Media is reader-supported indy media. To receive new posts and support our work, please become a free or paid subscriber. by Eric Lullove, Blue Amp Media Medical Contributing Editor The role of the U.S. Surgeon General has historically been the "nation's doctor"--a position built on the bedrock of rigorous clinical training, board certification, and an unwavering commitment to evidence-based public health. From C. Everett Koop to Luther Terry, this office has been occupied by individuals who, regardless of their political stripes, were fundamentally grounded in the reality of clinical medicine. Watching the recent Senate HELP Committee confirmation hearing for Casey Means, I couldn't help but feel that we are witnessing the erosion of that standard. | | ---|---|--- The Credentials Gap The most alarming aspect of this nomination isn't just the political baggage--it's the fundamental lack of clinical readiness. Dr. Means, a graduate of Stanford Medical School, never completed a residency program and is not board-certified in any specialty. Her medical license is currently held in inactive status. In the surgical and wound care world, we understand that experience is earned at the bedside and in the operating room. There is no substitute for the years of rigorous training required to earn board certification. When we discuss public health policy that affects millions, we aren't talking about "wellness coaching" or social media optimization; we are talking about life-and-death decisions, infectious disease protocols, and the translation of complex data for a lay population. Promoting an individual who walked away from clinical practice--and who has no residency training--to lead the U.S. Public Health Service Commissioned Corps is not just unconventional; it is a profound dereliction of the standards we should demand for the nation's health. Cliff 's Note: If you love fresh seafood, staying fit, and wanna help BAM grow --a trifecta!--please click on the link below and check out these delicious and health options. Thanks! SPONSORED Why More Seafood (and Less Red Meat) Might Be the Wellness Shift You 've Been Looking For | | ---|---|--- . Science shows that eating fish 1-2 times a week can support healthy heart and brain function, help reduce inflammation, and even may give your metabolism a little boost. Here 's what wild-caught seafood delivers: essential n‐3 polyunsaturated fats (specifically, the marine-based omega-3 fatty acids that nutritionists love so much!), lean and digestible protein, and a smorgasbord of nutrients. . Switching to seafood as your go-to protein is not just a shift away from a red meat rut. It 's a shift toward higher-quality nutrients and a more diversified diet for well-rounded health. The Hearing Performance: A Failure to Clarify The Senate hearing this week was not just "tense"; it was a masterclass in deflection. When pressed by both Republican and Democratic senators on critical issues--most notably the routine childhood vaccine schedule and the efficacy of the Hepatitis B vaccine for newborns--Dr. Means repeatedly retreated into the nebulous language of "informed consent" and "shared decision-making." While autonomy is a core tenet of medical ethics, the Surgeon General is not a private practice doctor discussing a single case with a patient; they are the chief spokesperson for public health. When a nominee for this role refuses to clearly affirm the safety and necessity of the routine vaccine schedule in the face of measles outbreaks, they aren't practicing "nuance"--they are signaling an alignment with anti-vaccine rhetoric that undermines the very institutions they would be tasked to lead. Dr. Means attempted to frame her skepticism as a push for "gold standard science," yet she struggled to distance herself from the disinformation that has plagued federal agencies for years. Her inability to provide a clear, unequivocal endorsement of foundational public health measures--despite being "grilled" by the committee--was, in my view, a failure to demonstrate the clarity, resolve, and professional authority the office requires. The Wellness "Influencer" Problem We are living in an era where "wellness influencers" often command more attention than peer-reviewed literature. Dr. Means' career--built on social media sponsorships, supplement promotion, and diet-centric messaging--is precisely the kind of commercialization of medicine that threatens to erode public trust. If confirmed, the Surgeon General will hold a massive megaphone. If that megaphone is used to advance the "Make America Healthy Again" agenda through the lens of a non-practicing influencer--rather than a seasoned, board-certified physician committed to the established medical consensus--the implications for our national health infrastructure are severe. We deserve a Surgeon General who leads with clinical, peer-reviewed evidence. We do not need an influencer whose primary background is in navigating the social media algorithm rather than the hospital wards. The Senate has a constitutional, moral, ethical and legal responsibility to reject her confirmation. Dr. Lullove is a Board-Certified Podiatric Physician and Wound Care Specialist based in South Florida. His expertise in public health policy, reimbursement models, AI models for physicians as well as researcher, investigator and subject-matter expert accounts for the bulk of his career work. | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

clean_body: Most retirees miss out on bigger Social Security checks—leaving billions unclaimed. | | | Don't want to receive our publications? | | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | Most retirees miss out on bigger Social Security checks—leaving billions unclaimed. **Behind the Markets**
Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

| | Fellow Christian,A thousand years from today, you will still be alive somewhere.That truth is rooted in Scripture. Ecclesiastes tells us that God has set eternity in the human heart (Ecclesiastes 3:11). This life is brief, but eternity is forever.When we truly understand heaven, it changes how we live, how we give, and what we value. Jesus Himself urged us to store up treasures in heaven rather than on earth (Matthew 6:19-20).In _A Place Called Heaven_ , I show how a biblical view of eternity brings clarity and urgency to our lives today.Dr. O. S. Hawkins wrote, "No one is really ready to live life to the fullest until he or she is ready to die."

Hi friend,
Last week, our team celebrated raising $1 million dollars since we launched this people-powered campaign.
I wanted to take this as an opportunity to express my sincere gratitude for your support, your generosity, and your commitment to our campaign. We’re building a winning campaign for everyday Coloradans, and it’s only possible because of you.
We’re entering the final month of the first fundraising quarter of the year, and the numbers we post at the end of Q1 will shape how national groups, political pundits, and journalists view this race.
With Donald Trump pulling his endorsement from Jeff Hurd and Republicans heading into a messy primary, March is our chance to prove that we have what it takes to send Hurd packing for good.
The earlier we build strength, the harder it is for Trump and outside groups to define this race on their terms. That’s why I’m asking you to chip in $10 or more today to help us start March strong and build the kind of report that shows we’re ready to flip this seat » [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/2603-q1-ak?&amount=10]

clean_body: [] Hi friend, Last week, our team celebrated raising $1 million dollars since we launched this people-powered campaign. I wanted to take this as an opportunity to express my sincere gratitude for your support, your generosity, and your commitment to our campaign. We’re building a winning campaign for everyday Coloradans, and it’s only possible because of you. We’re entering the final month of the first fundraising quarter of the year, and the numbers we post at the end of Q1 will shape how national groups, political pundits, and journalists view this race. With Donald Trump pulling his endorsement from Jeff Hurd and Republicans heading into a messy primary, March is our chance to prove that we have what it takes to send Hurd packing for good. The earlier we build strength, the harder it is for Trump and outside groups to define this race on their terms. That’s why I’m asking you to chip in $10 or more today to help us start March strong and build the kind of report that shows we’re ready to flip this seat » [] If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: DONATE $20 » [] DONATE $50 » [] DONATE $75 » [] DONATE $100 » [] DONATE $250 » [] OTHER AMOUNT » [] Let’s make sure the numbers reflect the movement we’re building across Southern and Western Colorado. — Alex [] [] [] [] [] Alex Kelloff for Colorado P.O. subject: Peter, I saved this message just for you

body:

We're honored to have you on our team, Peter.

# [Progress always seems impossible - until it doesn't](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=5963603&post_id=189571220&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg5NTcxMjIwLCJpYXQiOjE3NzIzODkyNTIsImV4cCI6MTc3NDk4MTI1MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTU5NjM2MDMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.640Wr5n5h0GKumTR4eLn69EtqDP7jx-73YDbIJ5OKwQ)
### What if everyone facing a hard challenge throughout history gave up before they got started?
| | [Michaela for Tennessee](https://substack.com/@michaelafortennessee)
---
| Mar 1
---
| [](https://substack.com/@michaelafortennessee)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=5963603&post_id=189571220&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg5NTcxMjIwLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc3MjM4OTI1MiwiZXhwIjoxNzc0OTgxMjUyLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNTk2MzYwMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.njFs3pARRoS_TkmuN_pRFClKR0t-9JqRqjIMsiEawW8&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=5963603&post_id=189571220&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg5NTcxMjIwLCJpYXQiOjE3NzIzODkyNTIsImV4cCI6MTc3NDk4MTI1MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTU5NjM2MDMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.640Wr5n5h0GKumTR4eLn69EtqDP7jx-73YDbIJ5OKwQ&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=5963603&post_id=189571220&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg5NTcxMjIwLCJpYXQiOjE3NzIzODkyNTIsImV4cCI6MTc3NDk4MTI1MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTU5NjM2MDMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.640Wr5n5h0GKumTR4eLn69EtqDP7jx-73YDbIJ5OKwQ)

| | [](https://substack..e9UvxoLAEMl1sKLw6tutVpicFZKiq1TaHw0-hj4jphc?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
